quest.title: Deliver Uncle Milton's Package
quest.description: Milton, Pellias's uncle, has asked you to deliver a parcel to his brother in Sandpoint.

notes.content: The package is a symbolic act, your uncle formally cutting ties with the 'main branch' of the family. Pellias has heard some whispers of the family drama, but growing up his parents and uncle were always quick to change the subject...

quest.title: Destroy the Horn of Rust
quest.description: The Horn of Rust is a powerful artifact, but as long as it exists there remains a chance that Xar-Azmak will return. Ferrumnestra has asked you to find a way to destroy the thing once and for all.

notes.content: As an Artifact, the Horn of Rust cannot be destroyed by conventional means. You must find a way to unravel the divine magic coursing through it.

quest.title: Escape Hell
quest.description: You entered a portal to Dis to defeat Xar-Azmak, but the doorway closed behind you. You must find a way back to the Material Plane or submit to a life living amongst devils.

notes.content: You've learned about a cult in the city that call themselves the Midwives. Rumor has it that they are able to provide teleportation services in exchange for large amounts of energy. How much energy and in what form? We'll have to seek them out to learn more.
Navigating infernal paperwork isn't the only way to escape. A powerful spellcaster should be able to open a tunnel to the Material Plane if you can convince them.
Hell's beaureacracy is a nightmare to navigate. Perhaps you can find someone willing to help you through it... for a price.

A Coarti named Ariuk has been assigned as your personal guide throughout the city of Dis. He can hear you whenever you call for him and seems to appear before you can even finish your sentence. He was sent by Erecura, the Queen of Dis, though why she's taken a particular interest in you is still a mystery.

quest.title: Find Ashka's mom
quest.description: When Ashka was little, cloaked figures appeared to take her away and her mother hid her. They took her instead, and Ashka has been searching for her ever since.

notes.content: Meitremar is dead, and before he could reveal anything about Thassilon's connection to the Denizens of Leng. Ashka needs a new lead. Avistan, the home of the human half of Jade's family, leads the Inner Sea in Ancient Thassilonian scholarship.
The Runelords of Ancient Thassilon allegedly had dealings with the Denizens of Leng. Perhaps their ancient knowledge can help you find them.
Over the years, Ashka has been able to deduce that her mother was taken by people called the Denizens of Leng. Who are they? What did they want with Ashka? Where have they taken her mother?

quest.title: Investigate Blu's orb
quest.description: Before the events at Rusthenge, Blu felt a pull towards some Ancient Thassilonian ruins. Investigating, they found a patrol of Xin-Edasseril's military cleaning out a trove of magical artifacts, the orb among them. What is it? Why does it pull at Blu's mind?

notes.content: Jade was able to determine that the Orb could be Activated, but attempting to do so backfired and shocked her with a bolt of electricity. What could it do if wielded correctly?
The orb feels... awake somehow. While it's mostly inert, sometimes when he holds it Blu gets the feeling that he's being watched.

ranks.name: Liked
ranks.effect: 10% discount on Healing items and services

ranks.name: Disliked
ranks.effect: The consortium is spreading petty rumors about you. You get a -1 Circumstance penalty to all attempts to Gather Information from groups you are not at least Liked by.

calendar.title: A Message in the Night
calendar.description: A dying Gorumite asks for help with his final breath; Iron Harbor is in danger.

calendar.title: Abjurant Day
calendar.description: Nethysian holiday devoted to magical protection and securing that which is dear to you.

calendar.title: Allbrith
calendar.description: What was once a day of bloodcurdling ritualistic sacrifice has become a day of temptation and celebration, ever since the half-orcs and Goblins of Katapesh popularized the celebration of the Demon Queen Lamashtu with secretive masquerades.

calendar.title: Archerfeast
calendar.description: Devotees of Erastil celebrate this holiday with archery contests and a celebration of the contribution of Hunters to their communities.

calendar.title: Ascendance Day (Iomedae)
calendar.description: Holiday marking the ascension of the goddess Iomedae after taking the Test of the Starstone.

calendar.title: Ascendance Night (Norgorber)
calendar.description: A holiday comemorating the apotheosis of the Reaper of Reputation.

calendar.title: Ascension Day (Cayden Cailean)
calendar.description: Celebration of the Accidental God's triumph over the Test of the Starstone.

calendar.title: Ashka's Birthday
calendar.description: One of Ashka's fondest memories is of the brithday tiaras their mother would give them, glistening with beautiful rubies...

calendar.title: Audrahni Arrives in Sandpoint
calendar.description: Audrahni arrives from Absalom, ready to perform the Call Spirit Ritual.

calendar.title: Blu's Birthday
calendar.description: A foundling raised by an order of monks, Blu's real birthdate is unknown. But on this day years ago, an Automaton delivered the child to the Order of the Platinum Band.

calendar.title: Candlemark
calendar.description: Holiday reminding the faithful that the Dawnflower's power to heal and redeem is always with her, even during the sun's weakest day. The day is also seen as a time to look forward to longer days.

calendar.title: Crystalhue
calendar.description: Shelynite holiday marked by the creation of artistic works, and the start of romantic courtships.

calendar.title: Currentseve
calendar.description: A somber festival, it was started by sailors seeking the protection of Gozreh, deity of storms, to ensure safe voyages as the spring fishing ventures began their preparations.

calendar.title: Day of Bones
calendar.description: Priests and worshipers of the Lady of Graves parade the bodies of the recently dead on this holiday, holding free burials afterwards.

calendar.title: Day of Order
calendar.description: Asmodean holiday recognizing Asmodeus slaying his brother Ihys at the dawn of this universe.

calendar.title: Day of the Inheritor
calendar.description: A holiday commemorating the church of Iomedae's adoption of the forlorn faithful of the dead god Aroden into their midst.

calendar.title: Elder Ordwi Found
calendar.description: The party finds Elder Ordwi

calendar.title: Evoking Day
calendar.description: Holiday marked by displays of fireworks and magical duels (both mock and real).

calendar.title: Feast of the Survivors
calendar.description: A Nidalese tradition celebrating the protection offered by Zon-Kuthon to their people for generations. Hyper-goth Passover.

calendar.title: Feast of Vigor
calendar.description: A communal, hedonistic celebration of pleasure led by the clerics of Calistria.

calendar.name: Festival of Making and Breaking
calendar.description: Artisans teach children to build something which, come evening, is then destroyed by teachers of magic in a vibrant elemental display.

calendar.title: Festival of the Witch
calendar.description: A tradition started in Irrisen, it is a celebration of all Witches and the refinement of their craft.

calendar.title: First Brewing
calendar.description: Cayden's priests use this holiday to bless the year's harvest of brewing crops and reflect on the hard work of the Accidental God's devotees.

calendar.title: Firstbloom
calendar.description: Firstbloom is a holiday celebrating the first planting of the agricultural season, and generally associated with the weather god Gozreh. It falls on the vernal equinox.

calendar.title: Izzy's Birthday
calendar.description: Izzy could never quite recall the specifics of when he was created, which is strange for Leshies. It's a puzzle to this day, but he celebrates his birth on this day, the anniversary of his first sold-out show.

calendar.title: Jade's Birthday
calendar.description: Half elf, half Varisian, 100% that witch.

calendar.title: Jestercap
calendar.description: A gnomish holiday celebrated by playing practical jokes.

calendar.name: Longnight
calendar.description: All-night festival in which revelers defy the darkness of winter and stay up to greet the dawn.

calendar.title: Merrymead
calendar.description: Merrymead was started by Druma's Caydenites, who had the brilliant idea to host a massive party to drink the remains of the previous year's alcohol in a symbolic act of drowning unfinished business.

calendar.title: Night of the Pale
calendar.description: On the Night of the Pale, it is said that the ghosts of those who died during the previous year manifest upon the world and come to visit the homes they lived in during life. Although some might think that the chance of seeing even the shade of a dearly departed one might be a blessing, the Night of the Pale is not a time for tearful reunions, for these ghosts, tradition says, do not return out of love for those they left behind but out of darker compulsions.

calendar.title: Pellias' Birthday
calendar.description: That religious hipster, worshipping some new god nobody's ever heard of...

calendar.name: Rememberance Moon
calendar.description: A comemorative holiday celebrating those that died in the Shining Crusades against the Whispering Tyrant.

calendar.title: Ritual of Stardust
calendar.description: Bi-annual festival held on the solstices, where the faithful of Desna sing songs through the night around bonfires.

calendar.title: Ritual of Stardust
calendar.description: Bi-annual festival held on the solstices, where the faithful of Desna sing songs through the night around bonfires.

calendar.title: Ritual of the Whip Sting
calendar.description: Calistrian day of vengeance. Any revenge taken on this day must be put fully to rest.

calendar.title: Swallowtail Festival
calendar.description: Priests of Desna release a wagon full of swallowtail butterflies in front of a crowd of believers. Worshipers consider it a good omen if a butterfly rests on him or her for a short time. The remainder of the day is spent in celebration and can include activities such as feasting, storytelling, and singing.

calendar.title: The Final Day
calendar.description: Cultists of Groetus perform an hour's silence at dusk on the last day of the year and seek guidance from their god about the End Time.

calendar.title: Transmutatum
calendar.description: Holiday celebrating transformation, both magical and metaphysical.

calendar.title: Turning Day
calendar.description: The changing of the year is celebrated by Alseta's faithful with the forgiveness of old debts and grudges, and embracing new opportunities.

calendar.title: Xar-Azmak Defeated
calendar.description: Party arrives in Dis, Hell

lore.title: Abadar
lore.summary: The Master of the First Vault — patient god of cities, law, merchants, and wealth, whose faith champions the expansion of civilization and lawful order across Golarion.
lore.content:
Abadar is the god of cities, law, merchants, and wealth, known to be a patient deity who maintains a strong neutral stance in his actions. He sets forth to expand civilization and order among the peoples of Golarion, believing that structured society and lawful trade are the foundations of prosperity and peace.

Abadar is allied with Asmodeus due to their shared appreciation for order and lawful societies, though the two have very different ends in mind. He has a good relationship with Irori. His faith is widespread in every major city of the Inner Sea and is welcomed at the Sandpoint Cathedral. His retired paladin Jasper Korvaski serves as Sandpoint's town planner — a man whose near-incorruptible character the Mercantile League values highly.

lore.title: Abraxas
lore.summary: The Master of the Final Incantation — demon lord of forbidden lore, magic, and secrets best left buried.
lore.content:
Abraxas is the demon lord of forbidden magic and dangerous secrets, patron of those who believe no knowledge should stay hidden no matter the cost of knowing it. His cults have always been small, scholarly, and catastrophic.

In the age of Thassilon, a sect in his service kept the catacombs that lie beneath what is now Sandpoint's cathedral. A small marble shrine to the demon lord still burns there, deep below the town, tended longer than anyone would credit.

lore.title: Abstalar Zantus
lore.summary: Sandpoint's high priest — a cleric of Desna who ministers to all faiths with steady, unhurried warmth.
lore.content: When the Sandpoint chapel burnt to the ground in 4702 AR, killing the town's priest, Ezakien Tobyn, his position in the town was filled by his top acolyte, Abstalar Zantus. Though Father Zantus is himself a cleric of Desna, his role as high priest of the since-rebuilt Sandpoint Cathedral requires him to be open to other faiths, and he is more than willing and open to assist followers of other gods with spiritual matters.

lore.title: Akoni
lore.summary: A gentle male dryad who rules the fey court of Akoni's Grove in the Tickwood.
lore.content: Akoni is a male dryad who makes his home in the Tickwood near Sandpoint, presiding over Akoni's Grove as lord of its fey court. His court is composed mainly of sprites and grigs, and he has long maintained a peaceful relationship with Sandpoint's hunters and nature-minded residents.

Akoni's Grove occupies the deepest part of the Tickwood, where the oldest and largest redwood trees stand. Here the dryad Akoni presides as lord of the wood's fey inhabitants, holding court over a retinue of sprites and grigs.

A family of redcaps — led by their self-styled patriarch 'Grandfather' Grustmier — had moved into the Tickwood and begun quietly poisoning the great redwood tree to which Akoni is bound, weakening the dryad until he had no choice but to comply with their demands.

Now chased off, the Grove is returning to normalcy, though Akoni still worries about what brought the redcaps to his woods in the first place...
lore.summary: The ancient redwood heart of the Tickwood, where the dryad Akoni holds court over sprites, grigs, and other fey — though recent redcap meddling has left its power dynamics dangerously uncertain.
lore.title: Akoni's Grove

lore.content: An unassuming, if strange, alchemist. Just as likely to offer a healing poltice as a deadly poison, depending on his judgement of your needs.
lore.summary: Sandpoint's alchemist — a poison dealer if you know how to ask hiding behind a respectable storefront.
lore.title: Aliver 'Pillbug' Podiker
Alma Avertin
lore.summary: Sandpoint Savories' master baker — twin sister of the slain sheriff Casp Avertin, and a mother worried sick for her adventuring daughter.
lore.content:
Alma Avertin has run Sandpoint Savories for decades, and her pastries are as close to a civic institution as bread gets. She is the twin sister of Casp Avertin, the sheriff slain by Chopper — a grief she carries quietly under the warmth she shows every customer.

These days a fresher worry sits on top of the old one: her daughter Arika took up the adventuring life against Alma's wishes, and their last argument ended badly. Arika hasn't been home since.

lore.title: Ameiko Kaijitsu
lore.summary: The former proprietor of the Rusty Dragon who left Sandpoint behind to reclaim her family's imperial birthright — she is now the Empress of Minkai.
lore.content:
Born Ameiko Kaijitsu, her true surname is Amatatsu — the last surviving rightful heir to the throne of Minkai. Before her ascension, she was best known in Sandpoint as the proprietor of the Rusty Dragon, the most popular inn and tavern in town, and as a former adventurer.

In 4707 AR, Ameiko lost both her father and half-brother during the goblin raids tied to the rise of Runelord Karzoug. In 4711 AR, she and a group of adventurers discovered her true heritage and embarked on a journey across the Crown of the World to Minkai, where she defeated the usurper Jade Regent and claimed the Jade Throne.

As of 4724 AR, Amatatsu Ameiko is Empress of Minkai. She still returns to Sandpoint on occasion for visits.

lore.title: Apsu
lore.summary: The Waybringer — the patron deity of all good and metallic dragons, and one of the oldest gods of the Great Beyond, who according to draconic myth shaped the cosmos from primordial chaos.
lore.content:
Apsu is the patron deity of all good and metallic dragons, and one of the oldest gods of the Great Beyond. According to the draconic creation myth, he was one of the primordial dragons who created the solar system and its inhabitants from the chaos of the Universe, making him a figure of cosmic significance in draconic religion.

Apsu is worshipped by the humanoid monastic order known as the Brotherhood of the Platinum Band, who venerate him as the source of righteous draconic power. His faith is small but sincere, and his followers tend toward the ideals of creation, guardianship, and the patient wisdom of ancient things.

Apsu's eternal enemy is Dahak, the evil dragon deity of destruction, treachery, and wanton violence.

lore.title: Arika Avertin
lore.summary: Alma's daughter — the sole survivor of the last adventuring company to brave the Pit.
lore.content:
Arika Avertin grew up over the ovens at Sandpoint Savories and chose a sword over a rolling pin. Her first adventuring company plumbed the Pit and met a grisly end there — Arika claims they ran afoul of the Sandpoint Devil, and she alone came back.

Rather than give it up, she joined a new company and headed south toward Magnimar. The Runewatchers, who keep track of local adventuring groups, can confirm that much but little more. Her mother wants one message carried to her if anyone finds her: 'Mother is worried about you and wants you to come home.'

lore.title: Ashava, the True Spark
lore.summary: Empyreal lord of dancers, lonely spirits, and moonlight — she who guides the restless dead home by the light of the moon.
lore.content:
Ashava is an empyreal lord — a celestial demigoddess — whose care is for spirits that have lost their way: the unmourned dead, souls too burdened or too bitter to pass on. Her faithful dance under the open night sky, and her rites are worked in moonlight, which her doctrine holds is the kindest light to call a spirit into.

Her worshippers are few but devoted healers and guides, and her faith holds that no spirit is beyond reaching — only beyond the patience of those who stopped trying.

lore.title: Asmodeus
lore.summary: The Prince of Darkness — the most powerful archdevil and the only one to claim full divinity — patron god of Cheliax and lord of all Hell.
lore.content:
Asmodeus is the most powerful of the nine archdevils that inhabit Hell, and the only one of Hell's rulers to claim full divinity. He is credited with penning the Contract of Creation, within which his followers believe is hidden the means for their patron's eventual rise to supremacy over all other gods.

Other gods find Asmodeus the easiest of the evil deities to deal with — they recognize his aid is not to be sought lightly, but he operates within predictable frameworks of law and contract. He is allied with Abadar due to their shared appreciation for order and lawful societies. He has an ongoing feud with Irori, who dislikes him for undermining others' hard work. He holds a great disdain for Grandmother Spider, who stole his keys and outwitted his retribution.

Asmodeus serves as the patron deity of Cheliax, whose ruling houses bound themselves to infernal power after the chaos following Aroden's death.

lore.title: Audrahni
lore.summary: An elven cleric of Ashava and old friend of Abstalar — a healer of lonely spirits, come to Sandpoint to lead the calling of Nualia.
lore.content:
Audrahni is a pale-skinned, dark-haired elf who dresses in somber colors and moves with an awkward gait and slumped posture rare among her kind. The awkwardness once extended to her personality, but her adventures alongside the heroes who faced Runelord Alaznist a few years ago restored her faith and left her confident and warm.

As a worshipper of Ashava — empyreal lord of dancers, lonely spirits, and moonlight — she is a gifted healer and offers her aid freely. She has come to Sandpoint at Abstalar's request to serve as primary caster of the Call Spirit ritual, for if any soul fits Ashava's definition of a lonely spirit in need of guidance, it is Nualia's.

Belor Hemlock changed his name from Viskalai — the Shoanti word for hemlock — as a gesture of belonging to the town that elected him in the aftermath of Chopper's rampage. His brother has never forgiven him for it.
lore.summary: Sandpoint's Shoanti sheriff — he took the job on the worst night of the town's history and never gave it back.
lore.title: Belor Hemlock
Bethana Corwin
lore.summary: The Rusty Dragon's quiet halfling manager — far more comfortable with linens and ledgers than with crowds.
lore.content: Bethana is introverted to the point of near-invisibility in a busy tavern, which suits her fine. She handles the maid services and room rentals and leaves the socializing to everyone else.

Nisk Tander's shop sells alchemical items and potions in impressive variety, piled on shelves in a system only Nisk understands. His prices are fair and his enthusiasm genuine; his quality control is another matter, and locals know that the occasional bottle from his shelves simply doesn't work. Those who want surer goods pay more at Pillbug's Pantry — and weigh that against the rumors about its owner.
lore.summary: A cluttered alchemist's shop on Raven Street — plentiful and varied stock, with quality that doesn't always match the label.
lore.title: Bottled Solutions

lore.content: Brodert studied with dwarven engineers at Janderhoff and catalogued ruins at the Founder's Archive in Magnimar. He's spent years insisting the Old Light was a war machine. The town is only now catching up to him, and he has mixed feelings about being vindicated this way.
lore.summary: Sandpoint's Thassilonian scholar — decades of study and no prestige to show for it, until recently when people started realizing he'd been right all along.
lore.title: Brodert Quink

lore.content: Brodert Quink's house is less a residence than a library with a bed in it. Sandpoint's foremost expert on Thassilonian lore — and self-appointed scholar of the Old Light — keeps decades of tomes, monographs, and half-finished theories here, and is always delighted by visitors who ask the right questions. Whether his advice is free depends on how much he likes you.
lore.summary: The comfy, book-crammed Tower Street home of Sandpoint's most respected sage.
lore.title: Brodert's House
Brotherhood of the Platinum Band
lore.summary: A small monastic order of humanoid worshippers devoted to Apsu the Waybringer, operating primarily out of Oppara and Absalom — and the order that raised Blu-Kakke before casting him out.
lore.content:
The Brotherhood of the Platinum Band is a monastic order of humanoid worshippers devoted to Apsu the Waybringer, the primordial dragon god of creation and patron of metallic dragons. According to the draconic creation myth Apsu is one of the primordial dragons who created the solar system and its inhabitants from the chaos of the Universe. The Brotherhood reveres him as the source of all righteous draconic power.

The small faith operates mostly out of Oppara and Absalom. They are an unusual congregation in that they are composed of humanoids rather than dragons, drawn to Apsu's ideals of good, creativity, and the long arc of cosmic order.

The Brotherhood raised Blu-Kakke after he was left in their care as a child by 'a man made of metal' from the north east. He trained with them until a connection to Blue Dragons — chromatic dragons associated with evil in orthodox draconic religion — scandalized the Brotherhood and led to his expulsion on religious grounds.

The Carpenter's Guild handles Sandpoint's building and repair work, from storm-damaged roofs to whole new houses. The dwarf Aesrick Battlehorn leads it, and his standards are why Sandpoint's buildings outlast its weather.
lore.summary: Festival Street's guildhall for builders — the place to go for a new home or honest repairs.
lore.title: Carpenter's Guild
Casp Avertin
lore.summary: Sandpoint's beloved former sheriff — Chopper's final victim, and Alma Avertin's twin brother.
lore.content:
Casp Avertin was sheriff of Sandpoint during the Late Unpleasantness, and died as he lived: standing between the town and its dangers. He caught the killer called Chopper in the act and was murdered stopping him, becoming the spree's twenty-fifth and final victim. Belor Hemlock, then his deputy, avenged him and succeeded him as sheriff.

His twin sister Alma still bakes at Sandpoint Savories, and the Town Watch still tells his story to new recruits.

lore.title: Cayden Cailean
lore.summary: The Accidental God of drink, freedom, adventure, and revelry — patron of Osprey Cove and one of the three deities who ascended to godhood through the Test of the Starstone.
lore.content:
Cayden Cailean is the god of drink, partying, freedom, and adventure. He is one of three mortals known to have passed the Test of the Starstone — a near-impossible divine trial — and ascended to godhood, though legend holds he was drunk at the time and can no longer clearly remember why he agreed to attempt it.

His faith is beloved by free spirits, travelers, fighters who prefer brawling to strategy, and anyone who believes that life is best lived without unnecessary chains. He has no patience for tyranny or the oppression of the weak. His followers in Osprey Cove, like Elder Ordwi, embody the town's laid-back, community-minded values.

Cayden Cailean is on good terms with Brigh (who appreciates the science of brewing) and counts Desna and Shelyn among his friendly divine neighbors.

lore.title: Charn Scarnetti
lore.summary: Titus Scarnetti's frustrated heir — an agitator against the cathedral's ritual, and an unwitting vessel for something far worse.
lore.content:
Charn has spent seventeen years watching the inheritance he considers his by right erode: the mill murder, the manor's ruin in the giant attack, the collusion scandal with the Bunyip Club. He blames his father's mismanagement and, above all, the meddling of adventurers. His campaign of fear against Abstalar's plan to call Nualia's spirit is equal parts insecurity and ambition — he means to build a name as a 'protector of the people,' and once adventurers start delving the Pit, his rumor-mongering finds a new target.

What Charn does not know is that the clockwork songbird's head he carries is a tether for the ghost of Jervis Stoot. Most days Stoot is only a voice at the back of his mind. But there are nights Charn cannot account for — nights he wakes exhausted, in clothes that are not the ones he went out in.

lore.title: Chask Haladan
lore.summary: Owner of the Curious Goblin — a retired adventurer who has spent seventy years loving books and shows no sign of stopping.
lore.content: Chask is old, unhurried, and surrounded by books he genuinely doesn't want to sell. He'll talk about almost any of them at length if you let him. He's kept the shop financially afloat for decades on the strength of a lucky adventuring career and a very frugal lifestyle.

lore.title: Cheliax
lore.summary: A once-vast empire that collapsed after the death of its patron god Aroden, Cheliax now rules itself through infernal pacts with devils and the iron authority of devil-worship — and its cultural shadow still falls over Sandpoint's most conservative families.
lore.content:
Cheliax is one of the most powerful nations of the Inner Sea region, a former empire whose reach once extended across much of western Avistan. After the death of the god Aroden, Cheliax fell into civil war and chaos, from which it emerged under the thumb of devil-worshipping aristocrats who bound themselves to the power of Hell. Today, Cheliax is ruled by an alliance of infernal-pact noble houses, with Asmodeus as the state's patron deity.

Chelaxian culture values order, hierarchy, law, and tradition — virtues taken to their darkest extreme in the service of Hell. The nation maintains the Hellknights, a collection of iron-disciplined knightly orders that enforce law with brutal efficiency, and its influence spreads through vassal states like Isger and former colonies like now-independent Vidrian.

In Sandpoint, Chelaxian cultural influence is most visible in the founding Scarnetti family, who cling to 'old values from Cheliax that are, in many cases, outdated.' The Chelaxian half of Sandpoint's founding population — settlers who came north via Magnimar — forms roughly half the town's human demographic.
Chopper's Isle is the small rocky outcrop revealed to be the home of Sandpoint's infamous serial killer.Nobody goes there if they can help it. Children dare each other to swim out to it.
lore.summary: A rocky island just off the coast — where Sandpoint's most infamous killer lived, and where the ruins of his home still sit, untouched.
lore.title: Chopper's Isle

lore.content: Cracktooth's is owned by Jesk 'Cracktooth' Berinni, who looks like a brawler and reads like a scholar. Nights when Jesk takes the stage to deliver his observations on Magnimar's politics are standing room only. The jagged teeth and thickset neck are somewhat misleading.
lore.summary: A tavern that looks like a rough-and-tumble dive but occasionally hosts the sharpest political commentary in town.
lore.title: Cracktooth's Tavern
Cyrdak Drokkus
lore.summary: Sandpoint's showman — theatrical, charming, and in possession of a past in Magnimar he'll reference constantly but never actually explain.
lore.content: Cyrdak runs the Sandpoint Theater with a flamboyance that has made him both beloved and indispensable. He's married to the paladin Jasper Korvaski, which he considers proof that Sandpoint was always where he was supposed to be.

lore.title: Dahak
lore.summary: The Endless Destruction — the evil dragon deity of destruction, treachery, and wanton violence, and the eternal enemy of Apsu.
lore.content:
Dahak is the evil dragon deity of destruction, treachery, and wanton violence. He is the eternal enemy of Apsu, the good dragon god, and their conflict represents the cosmic division within dragonkind between the metallic (good) and chromatic (evil) lineages.

Dahak's portfolio encompasses senseless destruction, betrayal, and the pleasure taken in causing pain and ruin. He is worshipped primarily by chromatic dragons and those mortals who seek power through destruction. His connection to Blue Dragons — whose lightning and proud cruelty align with his domain — is part of why Blu-Kakke's discovered connection to Blue Dragon power scandalized the Brotherhood of the Platinum Band, which is devoted to Apsu.

lore.title: Das Korvut
lore.summary: Sandpoint's smith — brilliant at his work and legendarily unpleasant about everything else.
lore.content: Das Korvut is bald, powerfully built, and constitutionally incapable of suffering fools. He produces exceptional metalwork. He will make you feel terrible about asking for it. The local children have a rhyme about him.
Daviren lost an eye to a goblin horsechopper during the Thistletop raid. He added eight goblin ears to his collection in the same fight. His feelings on the subject have not moderated since.
lore.summary: Security and muscle at the Rusty Dragon — and the man in Sandpoint with the most personal reasons to hate goblins.
lore.title: Daviren Hosk

lore.content:
Desna is the goddess of dreams, travelers, and freedom. She is one of the most widely worshipped deities in Varisia, particularly among the Varisian people who see her as a patron of the road and of personal liberty. Her faith emphasizes joy, exploration, and the beauty of the night sky.

The inclusion of a Desnan temple in the Sandpoint Cathedral was a condition of the compromise reached with the Varisian Council after the Scarnetti founding raid — a gesture of respect for Varisian nomadic culture. Father Abstalar Zantus, while a devoted cleric of Desna, keeps the cathedral open to all faiths.
lore.summary: The goddess of dreams, travelers, and freedom — one of the most widely worshipped deities in Varisia, with a particular significance to the Varisian people.
lore.title: Desna

lore.content: Deverin Manor houses Kendra Deverin alongside her brother's large and rambunctious family. Her sister-in-law Vana is a persistent presence who believes the family deserves more space, more luxury, and more acknowledgment than it currently receives. Kendra has been managing this with commendable patience.
lore.summary: Home of Sandpoint's mayor and the loud, sprawling Deverin family — perpetually crowded, perpetually political.
lore.title: Deverin Manor
The Devil's Disciples
lore.summary: A cult in the Pit's depths that venerates the Sandpoint Devil — their coming broke the Whistlefangs' hold on the upper caves.
lore.content:
Little is known of the Devil's Disciples in town, which suits them. They venerate the Sandpoint Devil and have established themselves in the Pit, and their arrival was catastrophic for the goblin clans that previously held its upper levels.

They are not merely hermits in a hole: the cult's agents are bold enough to operate inside Sandpoint itself when their interests demand it.

lore.title: Dis
lore.summary: The Iron City — the second layer of Hell and one of the largest prisons in all the cosmos, a metropolis of millions ruled with calculating cunning by the archdevil Dispater.
lore.content:
Dis is the second layer of Hell, and its name belongs both to the layer itself and to the immense iron city that fills it — a metropolis with a population of millions, a sprawling monument to what civilization becomes when order serves evil. It is one of the largest prisons in all the cosmos, and its ruler Dispater is its unyielding, merciless warden.

In contrast to the iron, fire, and stone that dominates most of Dis, a region called Eleusys — the personal palace of Dispater's wife Erecura — is filled with lush vegetation, a startling oasis of green within the infernal city. Erecura keeps her court there, attended by three norns who serve as her advisors and handmaidens.

Dispater is Hell's greatest jailer and politician, ruling its second layer, Dis. Known as the Iron Lord, Father of Dis, and First King, he is one of the most active rulers of Hell and one of Asmodeus's closest and longest-standing allies. He appears as a majestic, towering figure with rust-red skin, four horns, and a burning crown that hovers above his head — a fallen angel whose black wings he keeps retracted within his body at almost all times.

Dispater is a master strategist and expert in intrigue. He prefers to use his commanding presence, charm, and political leverage to achieve his ends rather than direct violence — but when violence becomes necessary, he reminds everyone precisely why he is feared. His symbol of office is an artifact known as the Eclipsing Eye. His religious symbol is an iron spike driven into a golden ring with a red and purple crown.

Alone among the lords of Hell, Dispater believes in the virtues of courtly love. He has taken three wives over his eons-long existence: the first is forgotten even by himself, her only memorial a shattered statue in his throne room. His second was Feronia, an elemental demigoddess, with whom he had a brief tempestuous relationship that produced the empyreal lord Ragathiel. His third and current wife is Erecura, a once-mortal demigoddess condemned to Hell for stealing the secret of eternal life from Pharasma — and whom Dispater sincerely loves.

Dispater's armies were responsible for the destruction of the demon lord Xar-Azmak, whose Abyssal realm Vlorus now lies unclaimed.
lore.summary: The Iron Lord — archdevil, greatest jailer and politician of Hell, ruler of Dis, and one of Asmodeus's oldest and most trusted allies. Calculating, patient, and absolutely merciless when provoked.
lore.title: Dispater
Elder Ordwi
lore.summary: One of the Elders of Osprey Cove — a cleric of Cayden Cailean, a friend of the natural world, and a warm ally of the party.
lore.content: Elder Ordwi is one of the governing council of Elders that leads Osprey Cove. She is a cleric of Cayden Cailean and a known friend of the natural world, embodying the town's values of freedom, community, and easy-going goodwill. She has become a personal friend of the party, and offered them hospitality — including lodging in the bar she runs — when they arrived in Osprey Cove.

lore.title: Erastil
lore.summary: Old Deadeye — one of the oldest gods still worshipped in the Inner Sea, patron of farming, hunting, family, and community, whose simple faith speaks to those who build their lives from the land.
lore.content:
Erastil is one of the oldest human gods still worshipped in the Inner Sea region, his religion dating back to before the Age of Darkness, when small farming communities and hunter-gatherers prayed to him for bountiful harvests and successful hunts. He leads his followers by example and good deeds rather than flowery rhetoric.

Erastil believes that groups work best when led by a good and strong leader, and he values family, community, children honoring their parents, and understanding the difference between work and play. His faith is humble, practical, and deeply rooted in the rhythms of rural life.

His faith is welcomed at the multi-faith Sandpoint Cathedral and provides aid to the poor in Magnimar's rougher districts.

lore.title: Erecura
lore.summary: The Queen of Dis — a once-mortal demigoddess condemned to Hell for stealing the secret of eternal life, now one of the most subtly powerful figures in the infernal city, and wife to Dispater.
lore.content:
Erecura is the Queen of Dis, the second layer of Hell, and the wife of its ruler, the archdevil Dispater. She is a demigoddess whose origins lie in mortality — she was condemned to Hell for stealing the secret of eternal life from Pharasma, the goddess of death and fate. Despite being an authority figure within Hell, her outlook and motivations are far removed from those of typical devils, and remain unclear even to her own husband.

As part of her nuptial agreements with Dispater, Erecura is allowed to keep a palace of her own liking — the lush, green-filled Eleusys — and to exercise power as she sees fit within Dis. Dispater sincerely loves her, often calling upon her to arbitrate disputes and delegating responsibilities of rule to her even when she does not ask. Asmodeus himself plans to rely on Erecura's savvy should Hell enter conflict with forces beyond its ordinary ken.

Her divine portfolio encompasses deduction, mind reading, subtlety, thriving in hostile environments, and the art of manipulating dangerous beings to one's advantage. Her worship is virtually unknown on Golarion and is strongly tied to the veneration of Dispater. Those few who do follow her tend to be soothsayers and non-evil rangers who admire her ability to maintain integrity and power within an environment of overwhelming evil.

Erecura prefers green and tan robes within her own palace of Eleusys, and dons black in her public role as Queen of Dis. She is attended by three norns — Felixia, Rowane, and Selieste — who serve as her advisors and handmaidens.

lore.title: Father Ezakien Tobyn
lore.summary: Sandpoint's previous high priest — a stern man of Desna who perished with the original chapel in the fire of 4702.
lore.content:
Father Ezakien Tobyn led Sandpoint's faithful before Abstalar Zantus, and died in the fire that consumed the town's original wooden chapel during the Late Unpleasantness. He raised the foundling Nualia as his foster daughter, and his public reputation was one of piety and discipline.

Those who remember him well speak more carefully. His strictness toward Nualia, some whisper, went beyond a guardian's firmness — and whatever the truth of it, the girl who grew up under his roof did not remember Sandpoint kindly.

Fatman's Feedbag is the Sandpoint establishment least interested in Sandpoint's reputation as a charming arts town. It caters to laborers, dockworkers, and anyone who wants to drink without being seen doing it.
lore.summary: The roughest bar in Sandpoint — cheap drinks, no questions asked, and a clientele that prefers it that way.
lore.title: Fatman's Feedbag
Ferrumnestra
lore.summary: The Lady of Rust — the Elemental Lord of metal, decline, and inevitability — an eternally wandering deity who opposes the resurrection of Xar-Azmak and has chosen Pellias Scarnetti as her champion.
lore.content:
Ferrumnestra is the Elemental Lord of Metal, known as the Lady of Rust. She is one of the two Elemental Lords of the Plane of Metal, her domain encompassing metal, decay, mourning, and the inevitability of endings. Her touch is that of slow decline — the rust of ages that eats away at even the strongest steel. While she has no particular desire to hasten any endings, she is there to consume the last crumbling detritus of what has fallen, leaving nonexistence in her wake.

Ferrumnestra keeps no permanent dwelling, always on the move as is her purpose. Her few devotees usually follow her example, traveling across the world and destroying the wreckage of ancient ruins in their wake. Her edicts are to accept the inevitable, clear what remains of that which has fallen to ruin, and usher the past into obscurity. Her anathema: to preserve knowledge that has outlived its time, or to restore an object or structure that has been reclaimed by the elements.

She is strongly opposed to the resurrection of Xar-Azmak, the dead demon lord whose realm Vlorus borders the Plane of Metal — viewing any attempt to restore him as a direct disrespect to her domain. She has chosen Pellias Scarnetti as her champion, a bond that his companion Jade Inferno has taken great interest in helping him explore.

lore.title: Sandpoint's Founding Families
lore.summary: Four powerful families from Magnimar — the Deverins, Scarnettis, Kaijitsus, and Valdemars — founded Sandpoint in 4666 AR, and their descendants continue to shape the town's politics today.
lore.content:
In 4666 AR, four powerful families from Magnimar consolidated their resources and sailed north to found Sandpoint. Each family brought a different industry: the Kaijitsus were glassmakers and jewelers, the Valdemars shipbuilders and carpenters, the Scarnettis loggers and millers, and the Deverins farmers and brewers.

Their founding was marred almost immediately. They arrived to find the land already inhabited by a large Varisian tribe. After some failed negotiations, a scandal leaving dozens of Varisians and Scarnettis dead rocked the negotiations. The resulting crisis threatened the establishement of the town all together until a compromise was reached: the Varisians received a sixty-year share of Sandpoint profits (later extended to 4726 AR) and the promise of a Desnan temple in the cathedral.

Today, the Deverins and Scarnettis remain Sandpoint's most influential founding families. The Kaijitsus departed when Ameiko Kaijitsu ascended to become Empress of Minkai. The Valdemars have effectively withdrawn from town politics following financial setbacks and the death of their Sandpoint patriarch.

lore.title: Garridan Viskalai
lore.summary: Owner of the White Deer — a Shoanti man who stayed in Sandpoint out of love for his family and has never quite forgiven himself for it.
lore.content: Garridan runs the White Deer with quiet efficiency. He regrets that his parents gave up their Shoanti roots to settle in Sandpoint, and resents that his brother did the same thing — and then renamed himself after it.

Sandpoint's main stabling facility is owned by Daviren Hosk, who named it with characteristic restraint. It functions as a fully operational stable and is well-maintained. Daviren's goblin ear collection is displayed near the entrance. Visitors are encouraged not to comment on it.
lore.summary: Sandpoint's stables — named with the particular enthusiasm of a man who really, really hates goblins.
lore.title: Goblin Squash Stables
Gorum
lore.summary: Our Lord in Iron — the god of battle, strength, and valor, whose worshippers seek glory through combat and who forms the spiritual backbone of the militaristic town of Iron Harbor.
lore.content:
Gorum is the god of battle, strength, and valor — known to his faithful as Our Lord in Iron. He is a deity of pure martial virtue, caring not for law or chaos, good or evil, only for the glory found in worthy combat. His followers believe that the greatest honor a warrior can achieve is to die in battle, and they seek out worthy conflicts with fervor.

Gorum has a herald known as the First Blade. His followers in the Iron Archipelago's Iron Harbor have made the fortress-church of Stonehome the center of their devotion — though that institution has recently been compromised by the cult of Xar-Azmak.

Gorum is viewed as disinterested in inventors, even of weapons, and has come into conflict with Urgathoa, who considers death by disease insufficiently honorable for warriors.

lore.title: Gorvi
lore.summary: Sandpoint's dung-sweeper — one of the town's few half-orcs, paid well enough to live better than he does, and seemingly content with his shack and his crew.
lore.content: Gorvi employs over two dozen youths to help manage Sandpoint's waste. He lives in a small, run-down shack despite earning a comfortable wage, and nobody has yet worked out whether that's philosophy or stubbornness.

Gorvi's shack looks one storm away from joining the refuse its owner manages. From it, the half-orc Gorvi runs Sandpoint's garbage collection with a crew of ruffians whose services — official and otherwise — are more valuable to the town than anyone likes to admit.
lore.summary: A dilapidated shack on Tower Street — home of Sandpoint's dungsweeper boss and his crew of useful troublemakers.
lore.title: Gorvi's Shack
Gozreh
lore.summary: The Wind and the Waves — an ancient, dual-natured deity of nature, weather, and the sea, worshipped by sailors, druids, and all who live close to the natural world.
lore.content:
Gozreh is an ancient deity, thought to have existed for as long as the wind has blown and the waters swelled. Their faithful are to be found in all places of nature, doing their will as they have for eons. Gozreh is dual-natured — representing both the wild sky and the deep sea, both calm and storm.

Gozreh is one of the deities who fought and helped trap Rovagug in the defense of all creation. Their faith is particularly embraced by those who live and work close to nature — sailors, druids, fishers, and healers of the wilds like Hannah Velerin of Sandpoint, who spends her mornings in the hinterlands enjoying Gozreh's bounty.

Grocer's Hall is where Sandpoint's food trade lives: an open facade hosting produce markets, with farming supplies sold in the back. The halfling grocer Olmur Danavakus runs it with unflappable good cheer and serves as the de facto link between the town and the hinterland farms.
lore.summary: An open-fronted hall on Festival Street — market stalls out front, farming supplies in the back.
lore.title: Grocer's Hall

lore.content: Hannah is trusted by most of Sandpoint and sits on the town council. She handles the anonymous threats showing up at her door with the patience of someone who has dealt with worse, and the caution of someone who knows that patience has limits.
lore.summary: Sandpoint's herbalist and healer — a town council member quietly dealing with a threatening situation she hasn't taken to the authorities yet.
lore.title: Hannah Velerin

lore.content: Hannah Velerin runs her shop and home as Sandpoint's primary source of herbal medicine and primal spellcasting. She spends her mornings in the hinterlands gathering ingredients and her afternoons treating patients.
lore.summary: Sandpoint's herbalist and healer — primal spellcasting, fresh remedies, and an anonymous harassment campaign that its proprietor is trying to handle without involving the sheriff.
lore.title: Hannah's
Hayliss Korvaski
lore.summary: Jasper's sister and fellow Abadar devotee — without her brother's restraint, and not at all sorry about it.
lore.content: Hayliss runs her shop with the same faith her brother Jasper holds, and none of his diplomatic temperament. She charges Scarnetti family members extra and considers the mayor's requests to keep the peace to be advisory at best. She has recently seen something unusual involving the Vinder girl, and she's looking for someone discreet to investigate.

lore.title: Hell
lore.summary: The Pit — an outer plane of absolute lawful evil, where obedience is honed to a razor's edge in service of Asmodeus and the torment of mortal souls.
lore.content:
Hell, sometimes called the Pit, is the ultimate expression of evil order in the Great Beyond. It is a plane where the weak are subjugated to serve the strong, where complete obedience and unquestioning faith are the supreme virtues. Its tortures are not the willful, random cruelty of the Abyss, nor the purely sadistic spite of Abaddon — they are precise, purposeful, and honed to a razor's edge in service of a single goal: bending the will of souls and the very architecture of creation to the glory of the lord Asmodeus and his perfect order.

Hell is home to myriads of devils and other evil outsiders, and is the final destination for countless mortal souls who made infernal bargains in life. Its architecture is one of endless iron, fire, and stone, shaped into cities, prisons, and fortresses of terrible grandeur.

Hell is divided into nine layers, each ruled by an archdevil. Asmodeus rules from the deepest layer, Nessus. The second layer is Dis, an iron city-realm ruled by the archdevil Dispater.

When Nualia's grave was found torn open, the tunnel beneath it led somewhere no one expected: a complex of worked-stone chambers far older than Sandpoint, dating to the age of ancient Thassilon. Goblin ghouls had nested in the upper halls, raising a bloody shrine to Kabriri on which they bundled Nualia's stolen skeleton.

The deeper chambers hold older mysteries — fire-blasted rooms, a shrine where an ancient flame still burns, and great slabs of rune-carved stone whose purpose not even the town's sages can yet name.
lore.summary: Ancient Thassilonian chambers beneath the cathedral and Boneyard — broken into by the goblin ghouls who stole Nualia's remains.
lore.title: Sandpoint Catacombs
The Hellstorm Focus
lore.summary: Great rune-carved slabs beneath the cathedral — a surviving piece of Thassilonian engineering whose purpose the town's sages can only guess at.
lore.content:
Deep in the catacombs beneath Sandpoint's cathedral stand slabs of ancient stone dense with Thassilonian runes, infused with magic that has outlasted empires. The device — if device it is — is connected to something the catacombs' ancient keepers called the Hellstorm Flume; a demon bound to the deep shrine has prayed for its reactivation since before Sandpoint existed.

Neither on-site study nor Brodert Quink's library can unravel it completely. The fullest surviving notes on the Focus lie elsewhere, in hands that have been studying it far more recently.

lore.title: Horn of Rust
lore.summary: A relic of the dead demon lord Xar-Azmak — a coiled iron horn bleeding rust, still radiating the chaotic power of its dead master. Ferrumnestra has asked the party to destroy it.
lore.content:
This rusted, coiled iron horn is sizable, appearing similar in shape to a ram's horn but measuring nearly two feet in diameter. Flakes of rust constantly fall from it, and it leaves hands with a rusty red stain that lingers.

The Horn of Rust was an instrument of Xar-Azmak, the demon lord of rust and corruption whose Abyssal realm of Vlorus borders the Plane of Metal. Though Xar-Azmak was destroyed by Dispater's armies long ago, his power persists in the Horn. Meitremar's cult made use of it in their rituals at Rusthenge, attempting to channel its remaining charge toward Xar-Azmak's resurrection.

The Horn cannot be destroyed by conventional means — as an artifact, it must be unmade through some other method. Ferrumnestra has personally asked the party to find a way to destroy it, viewing its continued existence as an open wound in her domain.

While the Horn's power has diminished over the ages since Xar-Azmak's death, those who carry it are still subject to its palpable chaotic influence. Creatures not sanctified Unholy are Enfeebled 1 while carrying or using it; those sanctified Holy are Enfeebled 2 and Stupefied 1. Worshippers of Xar-Azmak are immune to these penalties.

The House of Blue Stones sits quietly at the edge of Sandpoint's residential areas, a small monastery run by a single monk named Sabyl Sorn. It does not advertise. Those who seek it out generally find what they came for.
lore.summary: A monastery at the edge of town — home to Sabyl Sorn, a monk who keeps to herself but is worth knowing.
lore.title: House of Blue Stones
Ibor Thorn
lore.summary: Manager of the Sandpoint Lumber Mill — a man whose nightmares about the machinery are getting harder to dismiss as stress.
lore.content: Ibor keeps the mill running and himself together through sheer stubbornness. His nightmares about the logsplitter have crossed a line from concerning to alarming, and people close to him are starting to notice.

Ilsoari is the kind of retired adventurer who chose to do something useful with his time. He runs the school and orphanage with genuine care. His basement collection is extraordinary. And there's a dungeon he never got to finish exploring — the Pit — that he's never quite gotten over.
lore.summary: Headmaster of Turandarok Academy — a retired adventurer who runs an orphanage and keeps a locked museum of the things he killed, with one notable regret.
lore.title: Ilsoari Gandethus

lore.content:
Iron Harbor is the sister settlement to Osprey Cove, situated on the mainland beside the island that holds the Thassilonian ruin of Rusthenge. Where Osprey Cove is gentle and communal, Iron Harbor is gruff, militaristic, and fiercely devoted to Gorum, the Lord of Battle. The town's center of power is Stonehome, a structure that serves simultaneously as garrison and temple.

Recently, Stonehome and Iron Harbor have been infiltrated and partially taken over by followers of the dead demon lord Xar-Azmak, operating under the direction of Meitremar — a scholar and operative who serves Runelord Belimarius's government in Xin-Edasseril. The loyal Gorumites who survived the takeover of Stonehome were handed over to Meitremar's sinspawn ally Azomi.
lore.summary: The rougher, more militant sister-town of Osprey Cove — a garrison settlement devoted to Gorum whose Stonehome church-fortress has recently been infiltrated by a dangerous cult.
lore.title: Iron Harbor
Jargie Quinn
lore.summary: The one-legged, endlessly gregarious owner of the Hagfish — a champion liar in the best possible sense.
lore.content: Jargie Quinn is the kind of man who can hold a room just by opening his mouth. His hagfish Norah has made the Hagfish famous. How he lost his leg is a different story every time and somehow always entertaining.

Jasper handles Sandpoint's planning, zoning, and property disputes with the same nigh-incorruptible character that once made him a paladin. The Scarnettis have tried to push him out of town. He has declined to leave.
lore.summary: Sandpoint's town planner and retired paladin of Abadar — incorruptible in ways that make him both invaluable and occasionally inconvenient.
lore.title: Jasper Korvaski
Jervis Stoot
lore.summary: 'Chopper' — the reclusive woodcarver whose murder spree took twenty-five lives and gave Sandpoint its darkest memory.
lore.content:
For years Jervis Stoot was simply Sandpoint's eccentric woodcarver, famed for the wooden birds he carved into posts, eaves, and doorframes — having a 'Stoot bird' on your home was a point of pride. He lived alone on the small isle off the northern cliffs, and no one thought twice about him.

Then the killings began. Over the course of a single terrible month, twenty-five townsfolk died to his hatchet, each found with eyes and tongue cut away. Sheriff Casp Avertin caught Stoot in the act and became his final victim before the killer was subdued and slain. Beneath Stoot's home, the town found a blood-soaked shrine to some winged demon; they burned the cottage, tore down the stairs carved into the cliff, and have shunned Chopper's Isle ever since.

lore.title: Jesk 'Cracktooth' Berinni
lore.summary: Owner of Cracktooth's Tavern — looks like a thug, thinks like a satirist, and is funnier than anyone expects.
lore.content: Jesk is well read, sharp-witted, and slightly dangerous. His amateur political commentary on Magnimar's ruling class has made him a minor local celebrity. Do not judge him by his teeth.

Junker's Edge is the waste disposal point for most of Sandpoint's refuse, a stretch of cliff from which garbage is dumped into the sea below. It is overseen by Gorvi, the town's half-orc dung-sweeper, who employs a small crew of youths and lives in a shack on Tower Street by choice rather than necessity.
lore.summary: The cliff at the edge of town where Sandpoint disposes of its refuse — and where Gorvi, the town's half-orc dung-sweeper, holds quiet authority.
lore.title: Junker's Edge
Kabriri
lore.summary: Him Who Gnaws — demon lord of ghouls, graves, and the hunger that outlives death.
lore.content:
Kabriri is the demon lord of ghouls and graveyards, worshipped by those who feed on the dead — and by the dead who feed. His faithful haunt tombs and warrens beneath the earth, and his rites are scrawled in blood over stolen bones.

The goblin ghouls who tunneled into the catacombs beneath Sandpoint's Boneyard raised just such a shrine: a flat-topped boulder smeared with his runes, on which they laid Nualia's stolen skeleton as an offering to prove their worth.

Kaijitsu Manor stands as a monument to a family that made Sandpoint's glasswork famous and then departed for a far grander stage. With Ameiko Kaijitsu now Empress of Minkai, the manor is maintained but quiet, and the glassworks it once supported stand closed and rumored-haunted nearby.
lore.summary: The Kaijitsu family estate — largely empty now, with its most famous resident an empress on the other side of the world.
lore.title: Kaijitsu Manor
Kaleb Valdemar
lore.summary: A scion of the Valdemar family whose courtesies conceal ties to the Midnight Dawn — and an agenda in the Pit.
lore.content:
Kaleb Valdemar wears the family name well in public, but his dealings run darker: his name appears among Aliver Podiker's incriminating ledgers, tying him to the organization called the Midnight Dawn.

By the time anyone thinks to look closely, Kaleb is gone — he scrubs his manor of evidence, hires mercenaries, and heads for the Pit. Asking around town only confirms that nobody has seen him since.

Kaye runs her establishment with a shrewdness that extends well beyond the front door. She has a seat on the town council, a relationship with Belor Hemlock that the town politely pretends not to notice, and ears in places most people forget are listening.
lore.summary: Proprietor of the Pixie's Kitten — well-connected, quietly formidable, and in a long-running relationship with the sheriff that neither of them advertises.
lore.title: Kaye Tresarani

lore.content: Kendra didn't want to be mayor. She won anyway, and has been better at the job than most expected. She governs with a personal touch that the founding families find insufficiently aristocratic and the townspeople find refreshing.
lore.summary: Sandpoint's mayor — fiery-haired, sharp-minded, and genuinely liked by the people she governs, which her rivals find deeply inconvenient.
lore.title: Kendra Deverin

lore.content:
The Kindred Coast is the name for the coast of an island in the Iron Archipelago, a region that has come under the political umbrella of New Thassilon. It is home to the brother-towns of Osprey Cove and Iron Harbor, both founded by escaped slaves from the pirating hub of Riddleport who sought a new life free of the city's violence and exploitation.

The region is notable for its proximity to significant Ancient Thassilonian ruins, most notably Rusthenge. This has drawn the attention of Runelord Belimarius's government, which has begun extending its influence into the area through operatives like Meitremar.
lore.summary: A stretch of coastline on an island in the Iron Archipelago, home to the twin towns of Osprey Cove and Iron Harbor — and to the shadow of the ancient Thassilonian ruin Rusthenge.
lore.title: The Kindred Coast

lore.content:
Korvosa is the eldest of Varisia's three city-states, once part of Imperial Cheliax but now independent. The settlement originally served as a defensive position for settlers entering Varisia, growing over time into a cosmopolitan center of trade. Korvosa is built upon the ruins of the Ancient Thassilonian city of Xin-Eurythnia, once the capital of the realm ruled by Runelord Sorshen, the Runelord of Lust.

Korvosa maintains strong ties to Chelaxian tradition and culture, which puts it at odds with the more progressive Magnimar.
lore.summary: The eldest of Varisia's city-states, Korvosa was once part of Imperial Cheliax and retains strong Chelaxian traditions, built atop the ruins of an ancient Thassilonian city.
lore.title: Korvosa
Lamashtu
lore.summary: The Mother of Monsters — demon goddess of madness, nightmares, and monstrous births, and the favored deity of goblinkind.
lore.content:
Lamashtu is the demon queen who clawed her way to true godhood, worshipped as the mother and maker of monsters. Her faith celebrates deformity, madness, and the fecund horror of the wild places; goblins, gnolls, and worse creatures across Varisia raise crude idols in her name.

Sandpoint knows her mark too well. It was Lamashtu to whom Nualia turned in her despair, and Lamashtu's blessing that twisted the woman's arm into a demonic talon before the heroes of seventeen years ago cut her down.

lore.title: Larz Rovanky
lore.summary: Owner of the Rovanky Tannery — a perfectionist who produces excellent leather goods, keeps firing his employees, and sees nothing contradictory about this.
lore.content: Larz expects perfection. He has never met an employee who meets that standard for long. The leather goods are worth the wait. The wait is sometimes very long.

lore.title: The Late Unpleasantness
lore.summary: A series of dark events in 4702 AR — murders by the serial killer 'Chopper' and the fire that destroyed Sandpoint's original chapel — that the townspeople prefer not to speak of.
lore.content:
Referred to locally as 'the Late Unpleasantness,' a series of catastrophic events struck Sandpoint in the winter of 4702 AR. First, a string of gruesome murders was carried out by a killer known only as 'Chopper' took the lives of dozens of townspeople before being stopped during his last terrible night.

Shortly afterward, a great fire destroyed the original Sandpoint chapel and the surrounding buildings, killing Father Ezakien Tobyn. Tobyn was succeeded by Abstalar Zantus, who took on the task of rebuilding the cathedral and the faith of the people of Sandpoint

Magnimar stands as one of the two major city-states of southern Varisia, sitting in the shadow of the most awe-inspiring ruins of Ancient Thassilon: the crumbled cyclopean bridge called the Irespan. The city is ambitious and cosmopolitan, governed by a Lord-Mayor and the Council of Ushers, with a third, less official body — the Varisian Council — representing the nomadic Varisian people.

Sandpoint is technically a holding of Magnimar, though its isolated location means the city's direct authority is limited. The four founding families of Sandpoint — the Deverins, Scarnettis, Kaijitsus, and Valdemars — all trace their origins to Magnimar's aristocracy.

Magnimar is divided into numerous districts, from the wealthy Alabaster District to the criminal-ridden Underbridge slums. Criminal organizations such as the Night Scales and the Sczarni gangs operate in its shadows. The Pathfinder Society maintains a lodge here at Heidmarch Manor.
lore.summary: One of Varisia's two great city-states, Magnimar is an ambitious metropolis that casts its political shadow over Sandpoint and much of the Lost Coast.
lore.title: Magnimar
Meitremar
lore.summary: A scholar and operative of New Thassilon who is using research found in Rusthenge to attempt the resurrection of the dead demon lord Xar-Azmak.
lore.content:
Meitremar is a dangerous scholar who is picking up where his grandfather left off: attempting the resurrection of Xar-Azmak, the long-dead demon lord of rust who once ruled the Abyssal realm of Vlorus. He has found the research necessary for this project within the Thassilonian ruin of Rusthenge near Iron Harbor.

Meitremar is positioned as a member of Runelord Belimarius's government in Xin-Edasseril, giving his work the political backing and resources of New Thassilon. In practice this relationship is one of convenience — his sinspawn ally Azomi, while nominally loyal to Belimarius, is loyal only to itself.

Meitremar's cult infiltrated and took over Stonehome in Iron Harbor, subverting the local Gorum worshippers, before they were soundly defeated. Meitremar's soul has been taken by the leigons of hell.

A pale, viscous substance that has begun appearing in the deeper markets of Sekamina and, more recently, filtering up toward the surface. It has no taste. It barely has a smell — just something faintly mineral, faintly wrong.

Those who drink it sleep. And while they sleep, they dream.

The dreams are vivid beyond natural dreaming — coherent, persistent, strange. Dreamers report the same recurring imagery: vast dark spaces, slow movement through tunnels, a distant sound like breathing. Some wake disturbed but unharmed. A few return to it. A smaller number keep returning until they can't stop.

No one with any credibility will confirm where it comes from, only that it originates somewhere in the Darklands — almost certainly Orv, given the routes it travels. Who produces it, what it actually is, and what extended use does to a person remain unanswered questions.

It is not widely available. Yet.
lore.summary: A pale, viscous substance rising up from the Darklands — those who drink it dream vividly and strangely, and some keep drinking.
lore.title: Midnight Milk

lore.content: Madame Mvashti is one of Sandpoint's oldest residents, a Varisian seer whose reputation for genuine insight has outlasted the skepticism of every newcomer who dismissed her. She offers occult spellcasting and maintains a spare room for travelers, but those who come looking for a novelty fortune-teller tend to leave unsettled in ways they didn't anticipate.
lore.summary: The home and business of Sandpoint's Varisian seer — a source of occult spellcasting and lodging, and an old woman who has been watching this town longer than most of it has existed.
lore.title: Mvashti's Place
Naffer Vosk
lore.summary: The caretaker of Sandpoint's boneyard — a former smuggler who found redemption in Sarenrae, and tends the dead with fierce devotion.
lore.content: Naffer walks with an awkward lurch from a twisted spine he's had since birth, but he maintains the Sandpoint Boneyard in meticulous condition and rings the cathedral bells at dawn, noon, and dusk without fail.

New Thassilon is the returned civilization of Ancient Thassilonian peoples — survivors and time-displaced subjects of the old empire who have re-established a presence in Varisia. It is currently split between the rule of two Runelords: Runelord Belimarius (Runelord of Envy) and Runelord Sorshen (Runelord of Lust), who has taken a surprisingly different path from her former colleagues.

The return of New Thassilon has sparked renewed interest in Thassilonian history and ruins throughout Varisia. It has also created a new wave of worship for two long-lost gods: Lissala (goddess of runes, once the patron deity of Thassilon) and the Peacock Spirit (a mysterious god of success and prosperity).
lore.summary: The recently returned Thassilonian civilization, formed by time-displaced peoples, is now split between the rule of Runelord Belimarius and the surprising reformer Runelord Sorshen.
lore.title: New Thassilon
Nualia
lore.summary: The cathedral's tragic daughter — an aasimar foundling tormented by the town that should have loved her, lost to dark powers, and slain seventeen years ago. Her spirit has never found rest.
lore.content:
Nualia was a foundling raised by Father Ezakien Tobyn at Sandpoint's old chapel. Her celestial heritage made her an object of fascination to the townsfolk — treated as a living icon by some and a curiosity by others, and shown precious little simple kindness by anyone. She was presumed dead in the fire of 4702 that destroyed the original chapel and killed Father Tobyn.

Years later she returned transformed: sworn to Lamashtu, her left arm twisted into a demonic talon, at the head of the plot that brought goblins down on Sandpoint. She was slain by the heroes of that era seventeen years ago and buried in a lonely corner of the Boneyard, set apart from the 'respectable' graves. Father Abstalar Zantus has long believed the town owes her an apology it never gave — and now that her stolen remains have been recovered from the catacombs, he means to deliver it by calling her spirit home.

The Old Light is a ruined lighthouse that stretches above Sandpoint's coastal bluffs, a relic of the Thassilonian empire that predates the town by millennia. Most residents treat it as a landmark. Brodert Quink, the local Thassilonian scholar, has spent years arguing that it was never a lighthouse at all — that it was a war machine capable of projecting fire at range. Nobody believed him, for a long time. The return of New Thassilon and its Runelords has made people reconsider.
lore.summary: Sandpoint's ancient ruined lighthouse — a Thassilonian remnant that Brodert Quink insists was once a weapon capable of destroying anything within a mile.
lore.title: The Old Light

lore.content:
Streaks of blue shimmer within the smoky depths of this perfect crystalline sphere. Both the scintillating shade and emanations of an indefinable power suggest that something slumbers within. When all is quiet, Blu can feel it pulling at him.

Blu found the orb before the events at Rusthenge, drawn toward some Ancient Thassilonian ruins where a patrol from Xin-Edasseril was cleaning out a trove of magical artifacts — the orb among them. What it was doing there, and why Xin-Edasseril's forces wanted it, remains unknown.

Jade determined it could be Activated, but attempting to do so backfired and discharged a bolt of electricity. The orb's full capabilities are not yet understood. It feels, in some hard-to-define way, awake — sometimes, when Blu holds it in quiet moments, he gets the distinct sense of being watched.

Given Blu's draconic lineage and his unusual connection to blue dragons specifically, the pull he feels toward the orb is unlikely to be coincidence.
lore.summary: A mysterious crystalline orb streaked with blue light, currently in Blu-Kakke's possession — it pulses with electricity, feels disturbingly awake, and nobody knows what it fully does yet.
lore.title: Orb of Blue Dragonkind

lore.content:
Osprey Cove is a small, community-minded town on the Kindred Coast of the Iron Archipelago, founded by escaped slaves from the pirate city of Riddleport. Its people have built a culture that prizes personal freedom, communal support, and a close relationship with the natural world. The town is generally full of worshippers of Cayden Cailean, and its druids and farmers set the tone for a settlement that is relaxed, egalitarian, and occasionally eccentric.

Osprey Cove is governed informally by a council of Elders, currently including Milton Scarnetti and Elder Ordwi — a cleric of Cayden Cailean and a known friend of the natural world. The town stands in marked contrast to its sister settlement Iron Harbor across the water: where Iron Harbor is gruff, martial, and devoted to Gorum, Osprey Cove is gentle, communal, and welcoming to outsiders.

The Kindred Coast region lies close to significant Ancient Thassilonian ruins, most notably Rusthenge, which has drawn the attention of Runelord Belimarius's government in Xin-Edasseril.
lore.summary: A laid-back coastal town of druids, farmers, and free spirits on the Kindred Coast — a world away from the grim militarism of its sister settlement Iron Harbor.
lore.title: Osprey Cove

lore.content:
The Pathfinder Society is a globe-spanning organization based out of Absalom, the City at the Center of the World. Its members — called Pathfinders — travel throughout Golarion to explore, delve, and document the lesser-seen parts of the world, sending journals back to their venture-captains. The most illuminating of these journals are compiled into the ongoing Pathfinder Chronicles series.

In Varisia, the Society operates out of Heidmarch Manor in Magnimar, run by Sheila and Canayven Heidmarch. They maintain ties with local scouts and take an active interest in Thassilonian ruins throughout the region.
lore.summary: A globe-spanning organization of explorers and chroniclers based in Absalom, with a significant presence in Magnimar through Heidmarch Manor.
lore.title: Pathfinder Society
Pazuzu
lore.summary: King of the avian demons — demon lord of the sky, whose whispered bargains have damned more mortals than any army.
lore.content:
Pazuzu is the demon lord of the sky and of temptation, lord of the Abyssal realm of High M'vania and ancient, bitter enemy of Lamashtu. Where other demon lords conquer, Pazuzu corrupts: his favorite work is the slow ruin of a mortal soul, begun with something as small as speaking his name aloud.

His influence over the Lost Coast is older than Sandpoint itself — an ancient shrine to the winged king lies hidden in the rock of Chopper's Isle, and his servants have shaped more of the town's tragedies than anyone living suspects.

lore.title: Pharasma
lore.summary: The Lady of Graves — goddess of birth, death, and fate, who judges every departed soul in her Boneyard at the heart of the Outer Planes.
lore.content:
Pharasma is the goddess of birth, death, fate, and prophecy. Every mortal soul passes after death to her Boneyard — the vast plane of judgment from which Sandpoint's own cemetery takes its name — where it awaits her verdict and passage to its eternal reward or punishment. Her faith abhors undeath as a perversion of the natural cycle.

Her clergy preside over funerals and tend graveyards across Golarion, and her rites are woven into the Sandpoint Cathedral's care for the town's dead. A soul that dies with its affairs unsettled may wait long in the Boneyard's queue — and some, it is said, are too burdened by what they left behind to face judgment at all.

Pillbug's Pantry operates as a perfectly respectable alchemy shop under the ownership of Aliver 'Pillbug' Podiker. It sells medicines and potions that the town genuinely needs. It also, for the right customers who know to ask the right question, sells poisons and other morally dubious alchemical concoctions.
lore.summary: Sandpoint's alchemist shop — legitimate medicinal supplies on the shelves, and something considerably less legitimate in the back.
lore.title: Pillbug's Pantry

lore.content: Named for Das Korvut's beloved mastiffs, which lounge around the property at all times, the Red Dog Smithy produces excellent weapons and armor. Das has a foul temper, no patience for customers, and a habit of drunk midnight ranting that the town tolerates because the metalwork is genuinely superb. He's also illegally converted a vacant lot next door into a private cemetery for two mastiffs killed in the goblin raid, which has put the town planner in an awkward position.
lore.summary: Sandpoint's best smithy — extraordinary work, a terrifying owner, and several large red mastiffs who have better manners than their master.
lore.title: Red Dog Smithy
The Return of the Runelords
lore.summary: The remaining Runelords begin to awaken from their slumbers.
lore.content: In 4718 AR, after awakening from her slumber, Runelord Alaznist employed the Scepter of Ages to travel back in time and replace the history of Golarion with one where she rules over a New Thassilon stretching from the Lands of the Linnorm Kings to Ustalav and Nidal and beyond. Her efforts were foiled by a group of adventurers working under her rival Sorshen. By the time Alaznist was defeated, most runelords were also dead, except Sorshen and Belimarius, who claimed the land that would become New Thassilon.

Maver Kesk's shop trades in jewelry and art objects, and provides Sandpoint's closest thing to formal moneylending. Every couple of months Maver organizes a guarded caravan south to Magnimar to make deposits at the Bank of Abadar — a regular bit of work for trustworthy blades.
lore.summary: A squat stone shop on Church Street dealing in jewelry, art, and quiet loans.
lore.title: Rings and Things

lore.content: Risa built her tavern and her legend in equal measure. She travels the Dreamlands every night in her sleep — or she used to. Something is blocking her dreams now, and without them she believes she is fading.
lore.summary: Sandpoint's oldest innkeeper — a half-blind Varisian sorcerer whose tales of strange places are actually true, and who is quietly dying because she can no longer dream.
lore.title: Risa Magravi

lore.content: Risa's Place has been a Sandpoint fixture since the town's founding, built on the warmth and strange tales of its founder Risa Magravi, a Varisian sorcerer who spent her nights traveling the Dreamlands in her sleep. She's left day-to-day operations to her three children, Besk, Lanalee, and Vodger, but remains a fixture of the taproom. Lately she's been silent and listless — she hasn't been able to dream, and her appearances have become shorter and less frequent.
lore.summary: A cozy Varisian tavern famous for its founder's dreamland stories — now run by her children, while Risa herself grows quietly, dangerously ill.
lore.title: Risa's Place
The Rise of the Runelords
lore.summary: The emergence of Runelord Karzoug in Sandpoint.
lore.content:
Runelord Karzoug emerged in Sandpoint in 4702 AR as the initial one of Thassilon's seven runelords to awaken from millennia of stasis, kept just beyond the fringes of reality until an unwitting stone giant wizard triggered the mechanism that facilitated the runelord's resurgence. A group of heroes rose against Karzoug and his formidable giant forces, ultimately defeating him in 4707 AR and thereby reviving the legacy of Thassilon. As the revelation spread across Avistan that Varisia had once been the domain of a potent and ancient wizard empire that had faded into history, adventurers, scholars, explorers, and researchers arrived from distant lands to explore and delve into the enigma of Thassilon.

Leaders among Varisia's city-states became increasingly concerned. Seven runelords had vanished during Earthfall, and if the upheaval caused by a single awakening was so catastrophic, the potential ramifications of all the runelords rising from their slumber were ominous. Responding to their concerns, a fresh group of heroes embarked on a quest to locate and reconstruct an ancient artifact, the seven-pointed Sihedron, piecing it together from its fractured remnants.
