Choose one rider effect: you either become Charged, or you Discharge to increase the are to a 40ft line and the damage increases from d4s to d8s.

options.label: Become Charged
options.note: Off-guard vs electricity attacks and -2 to saves vs electric effects until it ends.

options.label: Discharge
options.note: Consumes Charged - the line extends to 40 feet and the damage dice become d8s.

powers.name: Polarize
powers.description: You electrically polarize the target, causing it to become charged as electricity surges through them, jolting them over and over. You deal 1d4 persistent electricity damage per two levels you have to a target within 30 ftt with a Basic Fortitude Save. On a failure they become Charged for 2 rounds. The persistent electricity damage from Polarize doesn't discharge their Charged condition. Choose one rider effect: you either become Charged, or you Discharge to increase the persistent electricity damage to 1d4 per level you have.

options.label: Become Charged
options.note: Off-guard vs electricity attacks and -2 to saves vs electric effects until it ends.

options.label: Discharge
options.note: Consumes Charged - the persistent electricity damage increases to 1d4 per level.

reactions.name: Upstage
reactions.trigger: An enemy attempts a skill check and doesn’t get a critical success
reactions.description: After your foe has tried their best, you show everyone how it’s really done. Attempt a check using the same skill that triggered this reaction. The DC is typically the same as the check the enemy attempted, though the GM can adjust this as usual.

degrees.Critical Success: You gain a +1 status bonus to attack rolls, Perception checks, saving throws, and skill checks until the end of your next turn.
degrees.Success: As critical success, except you gain the benefits only if the triggering creature failed their skill check.

roll.successNote: Applies only if the triggering creature failed their skill check (a critical success applies regardless).

feats.name: Celebrity Dedication
feats.description: The more people you have looking at you, the more content you are, and you take every opportunity to show up others when their performances are less than perfect. You gain the Upstage reaction. In addition, when you Earn Income, if the level of the task is higher than your level, you gain a +1 circumstance bonus to your check to Earn Income.

feats.name: Bardic Lore
feats.description: Your studies cover a wide range of topics. You are trained in Bardic Lore, a special Lore skill that can be used for any checks to Recall Knowledge. If you have legendary proficiency in Occultism, you gain expert proficiency in Bardic Lore, but you can't increase your proficiency rank in Bardic Lore by any other means.

strikes.name: Seedpod
strikes.description: Chuck one of your seed pods!

feats.name: Seedpod
feats.description: Your body produces a nearly endless supply of hard seedpods. You gain a seedpod ranged unarmed attack that deals 1d4 bludgeoning damage; these Strikes have the manipulate trait. On a critical hit, a seedpod bursts, issuing forth a tangle of rapid-growing vines that impose a -10-foot circumstance penalty to the target's Speed for 1 round.

feats.name: Eyes of the City
feats.description: You can track down targets with the help of locals. You can use Diplomacy or Society, whichever you're trained in, to Track creatures in settlements. You chat with locals to help follow the trail of creatures you Track. At the GM's discretion, there might not be enough people to speak with to keep following the trail.

feats.name: Quick Identification
feats.description: You can Identify Magic swiftly. You take only 1 minute when using Identify Magic to determine the properties of an item, ongoing effect, or location, rather than 10 minutes. If you’re a master, it takes a 3-action activity, and if you’re legendary, it takes 1 action.

reactions.name: Disrupting Performance
reactions.trigger: A creature you can observe within 30 feet takes a skill action with the Auditory, Concentrate, or Visual traits.
reactions.description: You make an awkward or bizarre sound, note, or gesture to foul the action the creature is attempting. Your reaction gains your choice of the auditory or visual trait. Make a Performance check against the triggering creature's Will DC. Creatures with less than a +0 Intelligence gain a +4 circumstance bonus to their Will DC. Regardless of the outcome, the creature then becomes immune to this reaction for 1 minute.

feats.name: Disrupting Performance
feats.description: You make an awkward or bizarre sound, note, or gesture to foul the action the creature is attempting. Your reaction gains your choice of the auditory or visual trait. Make a Performance check against the triggering creature's Will DC. Creatures with less than a +0 Intelligence gain a +4 circumstance bonus to their Will DC. Regardless of the outcome, the creature then becomes immune to this reaction for 1 minute.

feats.name: Multifarious Muse
feats.description: Your muse doesn't fall into a single label. Your muse is now also a Maestro, you gain that muse's unique ability, and can now choose feats that are available only to Maestro Bards.

feats.name: Eld Attunement
feats.description: The Ostilli bound to you has learned from absorbing the primal magic you naturally exude. When you make your daily preparations, you can attune your Ostilli to a single source of potent magic to which you have been exposed for at least half of the past 24 hours, granting you a new kind of magic called an Eld Power. You may use each one only once per hour as you replenish the Ostilli's store of magical energy.

freeActions.name: Harmless Bystander
freeActions.description: Roll initiative using your Deception rather than Perception. Any non-ally creature must attempt to Sense Motive against you to realize you are hostile to them and they cannot make reactions against you until you take a hostile action against the creature or its allies. If you are wielding a weapon or cannot reasonably hide yourself as a bystander, the creature gets a +4 circumstance bonus to their Sense Motive. If you are observed taking a hostile action you are automatically recognized as hostile. Regardless of the result of the creature's Sense Motive, once you are observed being hostile, non-allied creatures are temporarily immune to your use of Harmless Bystander for 1 day.

feats.name: Harmless Bystander
feats.description: You can pretend you have nothing to do with the conflict that is about to take place.

bloodline.name: Imperial
bloodline.description: One of your ancestors was a mortal who mastered magic. Such magical blood can remain latent for generations, but in you it manifested in full.
bloodline.blood_magic: Raw magic emanates from every cell in your body, protecting you. Until the start of your next turn, you gain +1 to either your AC or to Saving Throws.

reactions.name: Avoid Dire Fate
reactions.trigger: You fail or critically fail a check associated with your active Harrow Omen's suit
reactions.description: Treat the failure as a success or a critical failure as a failure. You no longer have an active harrow omen.

feats.name: Harrower Dedication
feats.description: You've dedicated yourself to the art of harrowing, a traditional Varisian fortune-telling practice using a deck of cards known as a Harrow deck. You can draw upon the power of the harrow to create supernatural effects based on the cards you draw.

feats.name: Otherworldly Magic
feats.description: Your elven blood runs strong enough to grant you magical abilities. You gain an extra Arcane cantrip.

feats.name: Alchemical Crafting
feats.description: You can use the Craft activity to create alchemical items. When you select this feat, you immediately add the formulas for four common 1st-level alchemical items to your formula book.

feats.name: Hobnobber
feats.description: You are skilled at learning information through conversation. The Gather Information exploration activity takes you half as long as normal (typically 1 hour instead of 2).

actions.name: Reach Spell
actions.description: You  can extend your spells’ range. If the next action you use is to Cast a Spell that has a range, increase that spell’s range by 30 feet. As is standard for increasing spell ranges, if the spell normally has a range of touch, you extend its range to 30 feet.

feats.name: Reach Spell
feats.description: You can extend the range of your spells. If the next action you use is to Cast a Spell that has a range, increase that spell's range by 30 feet. As is standard for increasing spell ranges, if the spell normally has a range of touch, you extend its range to 30 feet.

feats.name: Tell Fortune
feats.description: Whether through harrow cards, palm-reading, star-reading or some other method of divining the future, you are able to predict the futures of other people surprisingly well. You can spend 1 hour telling someone else's fortune. Attempt a Fortune-Telling Lore check against a hard DC of a target creature’s level, producing the effects of augury on a success with regards to the creature’s future. Regardless of the result, the creature is then immune to your Tell Fortune for 1 week.

actions.name: Split Shot
actions.description: You fragment a ranged spell. If your next action is to Cast a Spell without a duration that requires an attack roll against a single target, you can choose a second target within range. You roll a single attack roll and compare the result to the AC of both targets. This counts as one attack for your multiple attack penalty. To the second target, the spell deals half the amount of damage it would normally deal and has no effects beyond the spell's initial damage.

chain.modifier: Second target within range — one attack roll compared to both ACs (one attack for MAP); the second target takes half damage and no other effects

feats.name: Split Shot
feats.description: You fragment a ranged spell. If your next action is to Cast a Spell without a duration that requires an attack roll against a single target, you can choose a second target within range. You roll a single attack roll and compare the result to the AC of both targets. This counts as one attack for your multiple attack penalty. To the second target, the spell deals half the amount of damage it would normally deal and has no effects beyond the spell's initial damage.

actions.name: Harrow Casting
actions.description: You draw a card from your harrow deck just before you cast a spell to infuse your magic with its destined potential. If you next action is to Cast a Spell, the suit of the card you draw enhances the spell. If the card you draw matches the suit of your active Harrow Omen, the effect is enhanced. When you Harrow Cast, attempt a DC 11 flat check. If you fail, you lose your active Harrow Omen at the end of your turn.

chain.modifier: Draw a Harrow card before the spell; DC 11 flat check or lose your Harrow Omen at the end of your turn

feats.name: Harrow Casting
feats.description: You draw a card from your harrow deck just before you cast a spell to infuse your magic with its destined potential. If you next action is to Cast a Spell, the suit of the card you draw enhances the spell. If the card you draw matches the suit of your active Harrow Omen, the effect is enhanced. When you Harrow Cast, attempt a DC 11 flat check. If you fail, you lose your active Harrow Omen at the end of your turn.

feats.name: Glad-Hand
feats.description: First impressions are your strong suit. When you meet someone in a casual or social situation, you can immediately attempt a Diplomacy check to Make an Impression on that creature rather than needing to converse for 1 minute. If you fail, you can engage in 1 minute of conversation and attempt a new check at the end of the time rather than accepting the failure.

tenets: You must act with honor, never cheating, lying, or taking advantage of others.
You must act with mercy, showing compassion to those who deserve it and seeking to convert those who don't to goodness.
You must be righteous, standing against the forces of evil wherever they might hide.

actions.name: Channel Elements
actions.description:
You tap into your kinetic gate to make elements flow around you. Your kinetic aura activates, and as a part of this action, you can use a 1-action Elemental Blast or a 1-action stance impulse. Your kinetic aura is a 10-foot emanation where pieces of your kinetic element (or all your kinetic elements, if you can channel more than one) flow around you. The kinetic aura can't damage anything or affect the environment around you unless another ability allows it to. Channel Elements has the traits of all your kinetic elements.
Your kinetic aura automatically deactivates if you're knocked out, you use an impulse with the overflow trait, or you Dismiss the aura. Though you can't use new impulses while your kinetic aura is deactivated, ones you already used remain, and you can still Sustain any that can be sustained. Stance impulses are linked to your kinetic aura and end when the aura deactivates.

strikes.name: Melee Metal Blast
strikes.description: With a wave of your hand, you collect shards of metal from your aura and swing or hurl it. Make a melee or ranged impulse attack against the AC of one creature. Add your Strength modifier to the damage roll for a melee Elemental Blast. If you make a 2-action Elemental Blast, you gain a status bonus to the damage roll equal to your Constitution modifier. Your blast does piercing or slashing damage.

strikes.name: Ranged Metal Blast
strikes.description: With a wave of your hand, you collect shards of metal from your aura and swing or hurl it. Make a melee or ranged impulse attack against the AC of one creature. Add your Strength modifier to the damage roll for a melee Elemental Blast. When you make a 2-action Elemental Blast, you gain a status bonus to the damage roll equal to your Constitution modifier. Your blast does piercing or slashing damage, and has a range of 30 feet.

feats.name: Kineticist Dedication
feats.description: You gain the ability to gather and shape elemental energy. Choose an element: air, earth, fire, metal, water, or wood. You gain an impulse attack with the element trait from a gate of your choice.

feats.name: Deity's Domain
feats.description: You embody an aspect of your deity. You get the initial domain spell of the Metal domain as a Devotion Spell.

actions.name: Devoted Guardian
actions.description: You adopt a wide stance, ready to defend both yourself and your chosen ward. Select one adjacent creature. As long as your shield is raised and the creature remains adjacent to you, the creature gains a +1 circumstance bonus to their AC, or a +2 circumstance bonus if the shield you raised was a tower shield. (Pellias carries a reinforced tower shield, so the +2 effect is applied; the ward ends automatically when his shield is no longer raised — the GM should clear it early if the ally moves away.)

feats.name: Devoted Guardian
feats.description: You adopt a wide stance, ready to defend both yourself and your chosen ward.

feats.name: Nimble Reprisal
feats.description: You can use Retributive Strike at greater distance. You can use a ranged weapon to make a ranged Strike instead of a melee Strike for Retributive Strike. The enemy needs to be in range, but not in reach, and it must still be in your champion's aura. You can also make melee Strikes against enemies a bit farther away. If the enemy that triggered your reaction is outside your reach but is within 5 feet of your reach, as part of your reaction you can Step to put the enemy in your reach before making a melee Retributive Strike.

feats.name: Shield Block
feats.description: You snap your shield in place to ward off a blow. You immediately use your shield's Hardness to reduce the damage you would take by that amount. Your shield takes the damage that was prevented in this way instead.

feats.name: Untrained Improvisation
feats.description: You've learned how to handle situations when you're out of your depth. Your proficiency bonus to untrained skill checks is equal to half your level instead of +0. If you're 7th level or higher, the bonus increases to your full level instead.

feats.name: Natural Ambition
feats.description: Your ambition knows no bounds, and you strive to learn as much as you can. You gain an extra 1st-level class feat.

feats.name: Hefty Hauler
feats.description: You can carry more than your frame implies. Increase your maximum Bulk by 2 and your encumbered Bulk by 2.

feats.name: Hobnobber
feats.description: You are skilled at learning information through conversation. The Gather Information exploration activity takes you half as long as normal (typically 1 hour instead of 2).

feats.name: Titan Wrestler
feats.description: You can attempt to Disarm, Grapple, Shove, or Trip creatures up to two sizes larger than you, and you don't take a circumstance penalty when targeting a creature that's one size larger than you with these actions.

freeActions.name: Primary Threat
freeActions.trigger: Your turn begins and it is the first round of combat
freeActions.reminder: Primary Threat: Pellias Delays (GM moves him in the Foundry tracker). Enemies that act after he used Delay take a -1 circumstance penalty to attack rolls against his allies until the start of his next turn; a creature can spend a single concentrate action to remove it.
freeActions.description: You Delay while presenting an imposing afront to your enemies. Enemy creatures that go after you used Delay take a -1 circumstance penalty to attack rolls against your allies until the beginning of your next turn. Creatures can remove this penalty by spending a single action with the concentrate trait to do their own threat evaluation.

feats.name: Primary Threat
feats.description: When a fight begins, you make it clear you should be the one people are concerned about.

actions.name: Shard Strike
actions.description: Jagged metal shards form in the air and lash out in front of you. Creatures in the area attempt a Basic Reflex Save against your Class DC. Choose Shards or Spines. Shards deal 1d6 slashing damage in a 15-foot cone, and a creature that critically fails takes 1d6 persistent bleed damage. Spines deal 1d6 piercing damage in a 30-foot line, and a creature that critically fails is Clumsy 1 until the start of your next turn.

options.label: Spines — 30-foot line, piercing
options.note: Critical failure: clumsy 1 until the start of your next turn

riders.label: Spines: clumsy 1
riders.condition: clumsy 1 until the start of Pellias's next turn

feats.name: Through the Gate
feats.description: You have learned to channel the energies of the Plane of Metal more precisely; you gain a new Kineticist Impulse.

feats.name: Murmured Prayer
feats.description: A prayer under your breath to the appropriate god can guide you in times of trouble. You can cast Guidance as a divine innate cantrip. Once per day, you can utter a prayer to a particularly relevant deity as part of casting Guidance, and the bonus increases to a +2. You do not need to follow the deity to ask for their guidance, but if their goals do not align with you own, they may refuse to aid you. When you are Legendary in Religion, you can utter a prayer once per hour instead of daily.

reactions.name: Retributive Strike
reactions.trigger: An enemy damages an ally within 15 feet of you.
reactions.description: You protect your ally and strike your foe. The ally gains resistance to all damage against the triggering damage equal to 2 + your level. If the foe is within reach, make a melee Strike against it. Nimble Reprisal: you can instead make a ranged Strike (foe in range and in your aura), and if the foe is within 5 feet of your reach you can Step before the melee Strike.

reactions.name: Shield Block
reactions.trigger: While you have your shield raised, you take damage from a physical attack.
reactions.description: You position your shield to ward off a blow. Your shield prevents you from taking an amount of damage up to the shield's Hardness. You and the shield each take any remaining damage, possibly breaking or destroying the shield.

item.name: Light Hammer
item.description: This smaller version of the warhammer has a wooden or metal shaft ending in a metal head. Unlike its heavier cousin, it is light enough to throw. Cold iron weapons deal additional damage to creatures with weakness to cold iron, like demons and fey.

item.name: Longsword
item.description: Longswords can be one-edged or two-edged swords. Their blades are heavy and they're between 3 and 4 feet in length. Cold iron weapons deal additional damage to creatures with weakness to cold iron, like demons and fey.

item.name: Pick
item.description: A pick designed solely for combat has a sturdy wooden shaft and a heavy, pointed head to deliver devastating blows.

item.name: Aim-Aiding
item.description: Armor etched with this rune aids in routing ranged attacks aimed at an enemy around you. You don't provide enemies cover against your allies' ranged attacks.

item.name: Anticorrosion Oil
item.description: You can coat objects of 6 Bulk or less with anticorrosion oil. For 24 hours, the object takes half damage from acid and from all effects that specifically cause it to rust or corrode, such as contact with a rust monster's antennae.

item.name: Antidote
item.description: An antidote protects you against toxins. Upon drinking an antidote, you gain an item bonus to Fortitude saving throws against poisons for 6 hours.

variants.label: Lesser
variants.effect: You gain a +2 item bonus to Fortitude saving throws against poisons.

variants.label: Moderate
variants.effect: You gain a +3 item bonus to Fortitude saving throws against poisons.

item.name: Antiplague
item.description: Antiplague can fortify the body defenses against diseases. Upon drinking an antiplague, you gain an item bonus to Fortitude saving throws against diseases for 24 hours; this applies to your daily save against a disease progression.

variants.label: Lesser
variants.effect: You gain a +2 item bonus to Fortitude saving throws against diseases.

variants.label: Moderate
variants.effect: You gain a +3 item bonus to Fortitude saving throws against diseases.

item.name: Armor Potency
item.description: Magic wards deflect attacks, increasing the armor's item bonus to AC. The armor can be etched with property runes up to the potency's value.

variants.name: Armor Potency (+1)
variants.description: Increase the armor's item bonus to AC by 1. The armor can be etched with one property rune.

variants.name: Armor Potency (+2)
variants.description: Increase the armor's item bonus to AC by 2, and the armor can be etched with two property runes.

variants.name: Armor Potency (+3)
variants.description: Increase the armor's item bonus to AC by 3, and the armor can be etched with three property runes.

item.name: Backpack
item.description: A backpack for holding your gear

item.name: Beacon Shot
item.description: Ammunition (arrow or bolt); Activate (one-action, manipulate). The shaft of a beacon shot is studded with flecks of gemstones. When an activated beacon shot hits a target, it embeds itself into that target and spews sparks for 1 minute. If the target is invisible, it becomes merely hidden to creatures who would otherwise be unable to see it. The sparks also negate the concealed condition if the target was otherwise concealed. A creature can remove the arrow or bolt by using an Interact basic action and succeeding at a DC 20 Athletics check.

item.name: Bedroll
item.description: A compact mat, not too comfy but suitable for long journeys.

item.name: Blade Byrnie
item.description:
Instead of chain links, this +1 chain shirt is assembled from metal "leaves" that each resemble a small blade.

Upgrading the runes on the blade byrnie makes the daggers pulled from it more powerful. The daggers have a +2 weapon potency rune if the armor has a +2 armor potency rune, or a +3 weapon potency rune if the armor has a +3 armor potency rune.

actions.name: Draw a Blade
actions.description: Effect You pull a link from the armor, which transforms into a +1 striking dagger. The dagger disappears and reappears as a link in the armor after you Strike with it, or at the end of this turn if you don't make a Strike. You can Activate the blade byrnie in place of an Interact action to draw a weapon for abilities such as the Quick Draw feat.

item.name: Chalk
item.description: Suitable for marking hard surfaces temporarily.

item.name: Crafter's Eyepiece
item.description: This rugged metal eyepiece etched with square patterns is designed to be worn over a single eye. Twisting the lens reveals a faint three-dimensional outline of an item you plan to build or repair, with helpful labels on the component parts. You gain a +1 item bonus to Crafting checks. When you Repair an item, increase the Hit Points restored to 10 + 10 per proficiency rank on a success or 15 + 15 per proficiency rank on a critical success.

item.name: Crescent Cross
item.description: A crescent cross combines a small scizore with an arm-mounted crossbow apparatus that can hold up to three bolts at a time. It changes chambers automatically after you fire. To reload, you must load each bolt into the aparatus individually as its own action.

item.name: Dragon Turtle Plate
item.description: This harness of +1 resilient half plate is made from the shell plates of a dragon turtle and has the aquadynamic trait. To those who can craft with them, the dense organic plates on the dragon turtle's body are as valuable as the creature's hoard of gold.

reactions.name: Roll the Plates
reactions.trigger: A creature adjacent to you targets you with a melee attack.
reactions.description: You roll the hefty plates of the dragon turtle armor toward the attacker to gain a +1 circumstance bonus to AC against the triggering attack.

item.name: Dread (Lesser)
item.description: Eerie symbols cover your armor, inspiring terror in your foes. Frightened enemies within 30 feet that can see you must attempt a DC 20 Will save at the end of their turn; on a failure, the value of their frightened condition doesn't decrease below 1 that turn.

item.name: Eagle-eye Elixir
item.description: After you drink this elixir, you notice subtle visual details. For the next hour, you gain an item bonus to Perception checks that is greater when attempting to find secret doors and traps.

variants.label: Lesser
variants.effect: The bonus is +1, or +2 to find secret doors and traps.

variants.label: Moderate
variants.effect: The bonus is +2, or +3 to find secret doors and traps.

item.name: Elixir of Life
item.description: Elixirs of life accelerate a living creature natural healing processes and immune system. Upon drinking this elixir, you regain the listed number of Hit Points and gain an item bonus to saving throws against diseases and poisons for 10 minutes.

variants.label: Minor
variants.name: Minor Elixir of Life
variants.effect: The elixir restores 1d6 Hit Points, and the bonus is +1.

variants.label: Lesser
variants.name: Lesser Elixir of Life
variants.effect: The elixir restores 3d6+6 Hit Points, and the bonus is +1.

consumable.note: Restores HP — enter your rolled total

item.name: Eye Garnets
item.description: Large red rubies you pried from a statue of Xar-Azmak.

item.name: Flint & Steel
item.description: A camper's classic for starting fires.

item.name: Full Plate
item.description: Plate mail consists of interlocking plates that encase nearly the entire body in a carapace of steel. It is costly and heavy, and the wearer often requires help to don it correctly, but it provides some of the best defense armor can supply. A suit of this armor comes with an undercoat of padded armor and a pair of gauntlets.

item.name: Gecko Potion
item.description: A gecko potion is a sticky, tawny brown liquid with flecks of sand suspended in it. For 5 minutes after drinking this potion, your fingertips sprout thousands of microscopic, bristled hairs that cling to objects, granting you a +1 item bonus to Climb and Palm an Object, and to your Reflex DC against Disarm attempts.

item.name: Gourd Head
item.description: You have a large gourd for a skull, and since you don't have a physical brain, you can use the space inside your head. You can store a collection of up to 1 Bulk of objects within your head. The DC of checks to Steal objects from inside your head increases by 4. Additionally, if you store only one object within your head, you can draw it effortlessly into your hand as part of another action to use the object. Drawing the item grants this other action the manipulate trait.

item.name: Healer's Gloves
item.description: These clean, white gloves never show signs of blood, even after they're used to stitch up wounds or treat other ailments. They give you a +1 item bonus to Medicine checks.

actions.name: Healer's Touch
actions.description: Frequency once per day; Effect You soothe the wounds of a willing, living, adjacent creature, restoring 2d6+7 Hit Points to that creature. This is a healing vitality effect. You cannot harm undead with this healing.

item.name: Healer's Toolkit
item.description: This kit of bandages, herbs, and suturing tools is necessary for Medicine checks to Administer First Aid, Treat Disease, Treat Poison, or Treat Wounds. If you wear your healer's toolkit, you can draw and replace them as part of the action that uses them.

item.name: Healing Potion
item.description: A healing potion is a vial of a ruby-red liquid that imparts a tingling sensation as the drinker's wounds heal rapidly. When you drink a healing potion, you regain the listed number of Hit Points.

consumable.note: Restores HP — enter your rolled total

item.name: Holy Water
item.description: This vial contains water blessed by a benevolent deity. You activate a vial of holy water by throwing it as a Strike. It's a simple thrown weapon with a range increment of 20 feet. Holy water deals 1d6 spirit damage and 1 spirit splash damage. Holy water can damage only creatures with the unholy trait.

item.name: Horn of Rust
item.description: This rusted, coiled iron horn is sizable, appearing similar in shape to a ram's horn but measuring nearly 2 feet in diameter. Flakes of rust constantly fall from it, and the item leaves hands with a rusty red stain that lingers. While the Horn of Rust's power has diminished over the ages since Xar-Azmak's death, those who carry it are still subject to its palpable chaotic influences. If you are not sanctified Unholy, you are Enfeebled 1 while carrying or using the Horn of Rust; if you are sanctified Holy, you are instead Enfeebled 2 and Stupefied 1. It can be prepared as a staff, granting four spells.

actions.name: Blow the Horn
actions.description: Once per hour, you can blow on the horn to unleash a swirling aura of jagged, rusty metal shards that orbit you in a 5-foot emanation for 1 minute. The shards grant you a +1 item bonus to AC. Creatures and objects that begin their turn in this area take 6d6 slashing damage from the shards (DC 19 basic Reflex save). A creature damaged by this aura is exposed to a poten form of Tetanus that is a Level 5 disease and has a DC 19 Fortitude Save. Worshippers of Xar-Azmak are immune to this effect.

actions.name: Consume Corruption
actions.description: Once per day, you can place the horn's bell against a diseased creature and then inhale into the mouthpiece rather than blow into it. The horn attempts to draw out the disease, casting Cleanse Affliction with a counteract modifier of +11. If the Horn of Rust succeeds at countering the disease, it gains a number of staff charges equal to half the disease's level rounded down. If it critically fails, not only is the disease not countered, but you are exposed to the disease and must attempt a saving throw against it to resist contracting the affliction yourself.

item.name: Longbow
item.description: This 5-foot-tall bow, usually made of a single piece of elm, hickory, or yew, has a powerful draw and is excellent at propelling arrows with great force and at an extreme distance. You must use two hands to fire a longbow, and it can't be used while mounted. Because it's made for long range, you take a -2 penalty to attacks on creatures within 30ft with it.

item.name: Midnight Milk
item.description: The strange, swirling liquid in this bottle is said to give those who injest it a feeling of drowsy euphoria as it lulls them to sleep. It is a new drug, making its way into surface markets via trade routes in the Darklands.

item.name: Moderate Drakeheart Mutagen

consumable.note: +5 item AC, +2 item Perception; Dex cap +2 & −1 Will/Reflex/RK penalties (see effect)

item.description: Your skin grows scales like a drake, and your eyesight become sharp and your pupils slitted, but your mind and reflexes slow. You gain a +5 Item bonus to AC, a Dexterity cap of +2 (as usual, use your lowest Dexterity cap if you have more than one), and a +2 item bonus to Perception checks. If you're wearing armor, you still calculate your proficiency bonus to AC based on your proficiency in the armor you're wearing, even if the drakeheart mutagen has a higher item bonus. While under its effects, you take a –1 penalty to Will saves, Reflex saves, and all skill checks to Recall Knowledge. As a single action, you can burn through the remaining duration of the Mutagen to stride twice.

item.name: Moonlit Chain
item.description: This +1 silver chain shirt has a collar adorned with stitched images of the phases of the moon. You can see in moonlight as though you had low-light vision.

actions.name: Dim Sight
actions.description: Frequency once per day; Effect You touch the stitched image of the new moon on the armor's collar and suppress the dazzled condition for 1 minute.

item.name: Mother's Amulet
item.description: A blood-red ruby set in irridescent metal, the last gift your mother gave you.

reactions.name: Amulet's Abeyance
reactions.trigger: The target of your Exploit Vulnerability would damage you or an ally within 15 feet of you and you are holding your implement.
reactions.description: You forcefully present your amulet to turn away harm. You or a target ally within 15 feet gain resistance to all damage against the triggering damage. The resistance is equal to 2 + your level.

item.name: Mysterious Blue Orb
item.description: Streaks of blue shimmer within the smoky depths of this perfect crystalline sphere. Both the scintillating shade and emanations of an indefinable power suggest that something slumbers within. When all is quiet, Blu can feel it pulling at him.

item.name: Oil of Weightlessness
item.description: You can spread this shimmering oil on an item of 1 Bulk or less to make it feel weightless. It has negligible Bulk for 1 hour.

item.name: Padded Armor
item.description: This armor is simply a layer of heavy, quilted cloth, but it is sometimes used because it's so inexpensive. Padded armor is easier to damage and destroy than other types of armor. Heavy armor comes with a padded armor undercoat included in its Price, though it loses the comfort trait when worn under heavy armor. You can wear just that padded armor undercoat to sleep in, if your heavy armor is destroyed, or when otherwise not wearing the full heavy armor.

item.name: Potion of Waterbreathing
item.description: This filmy, gray potion reeks of ocean detritus and tastes even worse. After drinking this potion, you gain the ability to breathe underwater for 1 hour.

item.name: Quenching
item.description: This rune counters burning and corrosive agents. Armor with this rune reduces the DC of the flat check to end persistent acid or fire damage affecting you from 15 to 12 (7 with particularly effective assistance).

item.name: Rations
item.description: While they don't taste amazing, rations offer enough nutrition to keep you from starving. Each pack can feed a single person for a day.

item.name: Ready
item.description: A ready rune lets you don or remove the etched armor in only a few seconds rather than minutes.

actions.name: Don or Remove Armor
actions.description: Effect You don or remove the armor.

item.name: Reinforced Tower Shield
item.description: These massive shields can be used to provide cover to nearly the entire body. Due to their size, they are typically made of wood reinforced with metal. You provide cover for allies behind you when you raise a tower shield, and can increase the bonus to AC from +2 to +4 by Taking Cover after Raising the shield.

item.name: Resilient
item.description: Resilient runes imbue armor with additional protective magic, granting the wearer an item bonus to saving throws.

variants.label: Resilient
variants.name: Resilient
variants.description: This grants the wearer a +1 item bonus to saving throws.

variants.label: Greater
variants.name: Resilient (Greater)
variants.description: The armor grants a +2 item bonus to saving throws.

variants.label: Major
variants.name: Resilient (Major)
variants.description: The armor grants a +3 item bonus to saving throws.

item.name: Rope (50ft)
item.description: Sturdy rope for tying something (or someone) down.

item.name: Rust Scrub

consumable.note: Restore 2d4 HP to rust damage (GM adjudicates)

item.description: This salt scrub is infused with citron juice and alchemical ingredients that allow it to fight rust on metallic equipment. One application can be used to restore 2d4 Hit Points to an item that has been damaged by rust. The GM determines what portion of damage to an item was caused by rust if that value isn't known.

item.name: Rusty, Broken Full Plate Armor
item.description: Large red rubies you pried from a statue of Xar-Azmak.

item.name: Salve of Antiparalysis
item.description: Applying this filmy salve to a creature helps it overcome magical paralysis. Activate (one-action, manipulate); held in 2 hands. The salve attempts to counteract the paralysis (counteract rank 3rd, counteract modifier +22).

variants.label: Salve of Antiparalysis
variants.effect: The salve attempts to counteract the paralysis (counteract rank 3rd, counteract modifier +22).

variants.label: Greater
variants.effect: If the creature is petrified, it returns to normal, and for removing paralysis the salve's counteract modifier is +31 and its counteract rank is 6th.

item.name: Sanitizing Pin

activation.trigger: You attempt a save vs the initial exposure to poison or disease

item.description: This pin includes a piece of gauze wrapped around a dried herbal poultice. When you activate the sanitizing pin, the poultice dissolves and bolsters your body's defense against poison or disease. You gain a +2 status bonus on your Fortitude save against the initial exposure to the affliction. If you roll a critical failure on the triggering attempt, you get a failure instead.

item.name: Shadow
item.description: Shadow runes make armor lighter and softer. While wearing the etched armor, you gain a +1 item bonus to Stealth checks.

item.name: Shark Tooth Charm
item.description: This dried-seaweed bracelet is lined with charms shaped like small shark teeth. When you activate the bracelet, attempt to Escape using Acrobatics with a +1 item bonus to the check. If you roll a success, you get a critical success instead (if you roll a critical failure, you get a failure instead). If you fail the Acrobatics check against a grabbing creature, the creature must either release you as a free action or take 2d8 piercing damage as shark's teeth momentarily emerge from your skin.

activation.trigger: You attempt to Escape — gain a +1 item bonus to the Acrobatics check; a success becomes a critical success

item.name: Shuriken
item.description: This “throwing star” is a small piece of flat metal with sharp edges, designed to be flung with a flick of the wrist.

item.name: Skeleton Key
item.description: A grinning skull tops the bow of this macabre key. This key can be used in place of a thieves' toolkit when attempting to Pick a Lock, and it grants a +1 item bonus to the Thievery check. If the skeleton key becomes broken due to a critical failure on the check, it works as a normal thieves' toolkit and loses its benefits until repaired.

freeActions.name: Loosen Lock
freeActions.description: Once per day, the key casts Knock on a Lock you're trying to pick, granting a +4 status bonus to attempts to pick it.

secret.name: Shrieking Key
secret.description: A skull-topped metal shrieking key appears to be a skeleton key, useful in place of thieves' tools when attempting to Pick a Lock. A shrieking key has no activation, however. When you use it to Pick a Lock, the key emits a loud shriek audible for 500 feet despite ambient noise. Physical barriers still block or muffle the shriek as normal. You also take a –2 circumstance penalty to the Thievery check rather than enjoying a bonus. After you attempt such a check with the key the first time, it magically attaches to you, returning to your possession if discarded. To use another device to Pick a Lock, you must first succeed at a DC 20 Will save.

item.name: Slick
item.description: The defenses provided by this rune make it harder for foes to grab you. While wearing the etched armor, you gain a +1 item bonus to Acrobatics checks to Escape and to Squeeze.

item.name: Soap
item.description: Scrub a dub dub

item.name: Spacious Pouch
item.description: Though it appears to be a cloth bag decorated with panels of richly colored silk or stylish embroidery, a spacious pouch opens into a magical space larger than its outside dimensions. The Bulk held inside the bag doesn't change the Bulk of the spacious pouch itself. The amount of Bulk the bag's extradimensional space can hold depends on its type. You can Interact with the spacious pouch to stow items in it or remove them just like a mundane sack. Though the bag can hold a great amount of material, an object still needs to be able to fit through the opening of the sack to be stored inside. If the bag is overloaded or broken, it ruptures and is ruined, causing the items inside to be lost forever. If it's turned inside out, the items inside spill out unharmed, but the bag must be put right before it can be used again. A living creature placed inside the bag has enough air for 10 minutes before it begins to suffocate, and it can attempt to Escape against a DC of 13. An item inside the bag provides no benefits unless it's retrieved first. An item in the bag can't be detected by magic that detects only things on the same plane.

item.name: Spiked Steel Shield
item.description: Like wooden shields, steel shields come in a variety of shapes and sizes. Though more expensive than wooden shields, they are much more durable.

item.name: Spoiling Buckler
item.description:
A wooden buckler made of magically preserved rotting wood. It smells foul, but is a token of friendship from the fey of Tickwood.

Spoiling: Any food within 5 feet of it spoils at double the usual rate.

Bullet Guard: You gain resistance 5 against firearms and slings.

actions.name: Tumbling Tumbleweed
actions.description: Frequency once per day; Effect the buckler spawns a spherical wicker cage that tumbles at one creature within 30 feet. The creature must succeed at a DC 19 Reflex save or be carried 15 feet in a direction of your choice.

item.name: Sterling Artisan's Tools (Talismans)
item.description: An incredibly high-quality set of artisan tools, perfectly suited to creating Talismans. If you use them when you Craft a Talisman, you get a +1 item bonus to the check.

item.name: Striking
item.description: A striking rune stores destructive magic in the weapon, adding extra weapon damage dice on a hit.

variants.label: Striking
variants.name: Striking
variants.description: This weapon adds one additional weapon damage die.

variants.label: Greater
variants.name: Striking (Greater)
variants.description: This weapon adds two additional weapon damage dice.

variants.label: Major
variants.name: Striking (Major)
variants.description: This weapon adds three additional weapon damage dice.

item.name: Studded Leather Armor
item.description: This leather armor is reinforced with metal studs and sometimes small metal plates, providing most of the flexibility of leather armor with more robust protection.

item.name: Swallow-Spike
item.description: Your armor responds to your desire to break free of a creature grabbing you by growing spikes. Attacks made by armor with the swallow-spike rune apply the multiple attack penalty as if you had made them with another weapon.

reactions.name: Grow Spikes
reactions.trigger: You become grabbed, restrained, or otherwise held immobilized in a creature's grasp, such as by being engulfed or swallowed.
reactions.description: Your armor suddenly grows spikes, attacking the triggering creature. The armor makes a melee attack with an attack modifier of +14 that deals 2d6 piercing damage. If the creature is swallowing or engulfing you, the attack deals an additional 1d6 damage, and damage from this attack can cut you free if it equals or exceeds the Rupture value of the immobilizing ability. This attack gets an item bonus to the attack roll equal to the armor's item bonus to your AC and an item bonus to damage equal to double that amount.

actions.name: Renewed Assault
actions.description: Requirements You're being held immobilized as described in the rune's reaction; Effect Your armor attacks the creature immobilizing you. The armor makes a melee attack against the creature, as described in the rune's reaction.

item.name: Thieves' Toolkit
item.description: You need a thieves' toolkit to Pick Locks or Disable Devices (of some types) using the Thievery skill. If your thieves' toolkit is broken, you can repair it by replacing the lock picks with replacement picks appropriate to your toolkit; this doesn't require using the Repair action. If you wear your thieves' toolkit, you can draw and replace it as part of the action that uses it.

item.name: Torch
item.description: A torch sheds bright light in a 20-foot radius (and dim light to the next 20 feet) for 1 hour. It can be used as an improvised weapon that deals 1d4 bludgeoning damage plus 1 fire damage when lit.

item.name: Vangloris Tattoo
item.description: This tattoo features traditional Varisian geometry and is associated with divination and insight. Having one provides you with a +1 to Diplomacy checks when speaking to native Varisians, and allows you to cast Ventriloquism once per day. (Usage: tattooed on the body.)

actions.name: Cast Ventriloquism
actions.description: Frequency once per day; Duration 10 minutes. Whenever you speak or make any other sound vocally, you can make your vocalization seem to originate from somewhere else within 60 feet, and you can change that apparent location freely as you vocalize. Any creature that hears the sound can attempt to disbelieve your illusion.

item.name: Vocoder of Invisibility
item.description: A curious device found in a crashed spaceship in the deserts of Numeria.

actions.name: Unseen Din
actions.description: You engage a piece of the alien technology, producing a deafening sound as you become completely invisible. Because of the noise, you are only Concealed to other creatures unless they are deaf. This effect lasts until the end of your next turn.

item.name: Waterskin
item.description: When it's full, a waterskin contains roughly 1 day's worth of water for a Small or Medium creature.

item.name: Weapon Potency
item.description: Magic deepens the weapon's lethality, increasing its item bonus to attack rolls. The weapon can be etched with property runes up to the potency's value.

variants.name: Weapon Potency (+1)
variants.description: Increase the weapon's item bonus to attack rolls by 1. The weapon can be etched with one property rune.

variants.name: Weapon Potency (+2)
variants.description: Increase the weapon's item bonus to attack rolls by 2, and the weapon can be etched with two property runes.

variants.name: Weapon Potency (+3)
variants.description: Increase the weapon's item bonus to attack rolls by 3, and the weapon can be etched with three property runes.

item.name: Wisp Chain
item.description: This +1 resilient chain shirt is made of small, interlocking currents of wind carefully woven together. While the links of air don't jingle against each other like chain links might, the tiny cyclones nevertheless create a blustering howl akin to standing at the center of a storm. A creature that ends its turn adjacent to you must attempt a DC 23 Fortitude save. On a failure, it becomes deafened until it moves away from you.

actions.name: Slicing Links
actions.description: Frequency once per day; Effect You unbind the currents that form your armor and release them as cutting whorls of air that slice into creatures in a 60-foot cone. Creatures in the area take 6d6 slashing damage with a DC 25 basic Fortitude save. A creature that fails its save is also pushed 10 feet (or 20 feet on a critical failure). You lose the AC bonus of your armor until the end of your turn, when the air currents reform the wisp chain around you.

aura.effect: it becomes deafened until it moves away from you

item.name: Wolf Fang
item.description: This wolf canine is bound in a strip of leather and tied to a buckle or strap on a suit of armor. When the fang is affixed to a weapon and you succesfully trip a foe, you can activate it to deal bludgeoning damage equal to your Strength modifier to the target of your Trip.

item.name: Xanderghul's Flawless Hammer
item.description: At the height of the ancient Thassilonian Empire Emperor Xin crafted seven Alara'quin, magical weapons of extraordinary power. Xanderghul's Flawless Hammer was the symbol of office for Xanderghul, the Runelord of Pride.

reactions.name: Dominant Emotion (Pride)
reactions.trigger: You would be adversely affected by an emotion effect
reactions.description: The hammer tries to neutralize the triggering effect with its own emotion, attempting a counteract check. If the check succeeds, you feel a sudden rush of intense pride and the counteracted effect doesn't take hold of you.

staff.name: Xanderghul's Flawless Hammer
staff.description: At the height of the ancient Thassilonian Empire Emperor Xin crafted seven Alara'quin, magical weapons of extraordinary power. Xanderghul's Flawless Hammer was the symbol of office for Xanderghul, the Runelord of Pride.

spell.name: Ancestral Memories
spell.description: The memories of long-dead spellcasters grant you their knowledge, making your spells more formidable. You gain either a +1 status bonus to the next spell attack roll you attempt before the end of your turn or an enemy within 60 feet takes a –1 status penalty to the next saving throw they attempt against a spell you cast before the end of your turn.

heightened.5th: The bonus increases to +2 or the penalty increases to –2.
heightened.8th: The bonus increases to +3 or the penalty increases to –3.

spell.name: Blazing Bolt
spell.description: You fire a ray of heat and flame. Make a spell attack roll against a single creature. On a hit, the target takes 2d6 fire damage, and on a critical hit, the target takes double damage. For each additional action you use when Casting the Spell, you can fire an additional ray at a different target, to a maximum of three rays targeting three different targets for 3 actions. These attacks each increase your multiple attack penalty, but you don't increase your multiple attack penalty until after you make all the spell attack rolls for blazing bolt. If you spend 2 or more actions Casting the Spell, the damage increases to 4d6 fire damage on a hit, and it still deals double damage on a critical hit.

heightened.+1: The damage to each target increases by 1d6 for the 1-action version, or by 2d6 for the 2- and 3-action versions.

spell.name: Bless
spell.description: Blessings from beyond help your companions strike true. You and your allies gain a +1 status bonus to attack rolls while within the emanation. Once per round on subsequent turns, you can Sustain the spell to increase the emanation's radius by 10 feet. Bless can counteract bane.

spell.name: Breathe Fire
spell.description: A gout of flame sprays from your mouth. You deal 2d6 fire damage to creatures in the area with a basic Reflex save.

spell.name: Charitable Urge
spell.description: You speak on the virtue of charity, compelling the target to give away its possessions. The target must attempt a Will save. If the target has no items on its person, the spell fails.

degrees.Success: The target is stunned 1 as it wrestles with the urge.
degrees.Failure: On its next turn, before it does anything else, the target must present the nearest creature with an item in its possession; the target chooses which item to give, and if the only item it has is one that it's currently using to defend itself, such as a weapon during a combat encounter, it can choose to be stunned for 1 round instead of giving up the item. This might require the target to Interact to retrieve an item or move to reach the nearest creature, and passing the item off requires an Interact action as normal.
degrees.Critical Failure: As failure, except the duration is 4 rounds, and the target must repeat the effects of a failure on each of its turns. At the end of each of its turns, the target can attempt a new Will save to reduce the remaining duration by 1 round, ending the effects entirely on a critical success.

spell.name: Cleanse Affliction
spell.description: Gentle restorative magic pushes back the effects of toxins and more complex maladies. Choose an affliction on the target, such as a curse, disease, or poison. If it has advanced past stage one, reduce the stage by one. This reduction can be applied only once to a given case of an affliction, with the case ending when it's completely cured. Although the reduction can't occur again, heightened versions of this spell attempt to counteract with each casting.

heightened.3rd: Attempt to counteract the affliction if it is a disease or poison.
heightened.5th: Attempt to counteract the affliction if it is a curse, disease, or poison.

spell.name: Command
spell.description: You shout a command that's hard to ignore. You can command the target to approach you, run away, release what it's holding, fall prone, or stand in place. The creature must succeed at a Will save or follow your command on its next turn. Some commands might require the target to Seek to approach you.

degrees.Failure: The creature must spend the first action on its next turn to obey your command.
degrees.Critical Failure: The target must use all its actions on its next turn to obey your command.

heightened.5th: You can target up to 10 creatures.

spell.name: Counter Performance
spell.trigger: You or an ally within 60 feet would be affected by an auditory or visual effect.
spell.description: Your performance protects you and your allies. Roll a Performance check for a type you know—it must be an auditory performance if the trigger was auditory or a visual one for a visual trigger, and this action gains the traits for the type of performance (page 243). You and allies in the area can use the better result between your Performance check and the saving throw.

spell.name: Create Food
spell.description: You create enough food to feed six Medium creatures for a day. This food is bland and unappealing, but it is nourishing. After 1 day, if no one has eaten the food, it decays and becomes inedible. Most Small creatures eat one-quarter as much as a Medium creature (one-sixteenth as much for most Tiny creatures), and most Large creatures eat 10 times as much (100 times as much for Huge creatures and so on).

spell.name: Daze
spell.description: You cloud the target's mind and daze it with a mental jolt. The jolt deals 1d6 mental damage; the target must attempt a basic Will save. If the target critically fails the save, it is also stunned 1.

spell.name: Detect Magic
spell.description: You send out a pulse that registers the presence of magic. You receive no information beyond the presence or absence of magic. You can choose to ignore magic you're fully aware of, such as the magic items and ongoing spells of you and your allies. You detect illusion magic only if that magic's effect has a lower rank than the rank of your detect magic spell. However, items that have an illusion aura but aren't deceptive in appearance (such as an invisibility potion) typically are detected normally.

heightened.3rd: You learn the rank or level of the most powerful magical effect the spell detects, as determined by the GM.
heightened.4th: As 3rd rank, but you also pinpoint the source of the highest-rank magic. Like for an imprecise sense, you don't learn the exact location, but can narrow down the source to within a 5-foot cube (or the nearest if larger than that).

spell.name: Dispel Magic
spell.targets: 1 spell effect or unattended magic item
spell.description: You unravel the magic behind a spell or effect. Attempt a counteract check against the target. If you successfully counteract a magic item, the item becomes a mundane item of its type for 10 minutes. This doesn't change the item's non-magical properties. If the target is an artifact or similar item, you automatically fail.

spell.name: Dizzying Colors
spell.description: You unleash a swirling multitude of colors that overwhelms creatures based on their Will saves.

degrees.Success: The creature is dazzled for 1 round.
degrees.Failure: The creature stunned 1, blinded for 1 round, and dazzled for 1 minute.
degrees.Critical Failure: The creature is stunned for 1 round and blinded for 1 minute.

spell.name: Electric Arc
spell.description: An arc of lightning leaps from one target to another. You deal 1d4+4 electricity damage to the target creatures. The targets must attempt a basic Reflex save.

spell.name: Fashionista
spell.description: The target's clothes are transformed into ostentatious attire that epitomizes high-end local fashion. No details of the target's appearance transform other than their clothes, so their weapons or armor remain unchanged in appearance. The target gains a +1 status bonus on Deception checks to Make a Distraction. You can Dismiss this spell. At the end of the spell's duration, the target's clothes revert to their original appearance.

heightened.+2: The status bonus increases by 1, to a maximum of +4 at 7th level.

spell.name: Fear
spell.description: You plant fear in the target; it must attempt a Will save.

degrees.Critical Failure: The target is frightened 3 and fleeing for 1 round.

heightened.3rd: You can target up to five creatures.

spell.name: Figment
spell.description: You create a simple illusory sound or vision. A sound adds the auditory trait to the spell and the sound can't include intelligible words or elaborate music. A vision adds the visual trait, can be no larger than a 5-foot cube, and is clearly crude and undetailed if viewed from within 15 feet. When you Cast or Sustain the Spell, you can attempt to Create a Diversion with the illusion, gaining a +2 circumstance bonus to your Deception check. If the attempt fails against a creature, that creature disbelieves the figment.

spell.name: Forbidding Ward
spell.description: You ward an ally against the attacks and hostile spells from the target enemy. The target ally gains a +1 status bonus to Armor Class and saving throws against the target enemy's attacks, spells, and other effects.

spell.name: Force Barrage
spell.description: You fire a shard of solidified magic toward a creature that you can see. It automatically hits and deals 1d4+1 force damage. For each additional action you use when Casting the Spell, increase the number of shards you shoot by one, to a maximum of three shards for 3 actions. You choose the target for each shard individually. If you shoot more than one shard at the same target, combine the damage before applying bonuses or penalties to damage, resistances, weaknesses, and so forth.

heightened.+2: You fire one additional missile with each action you spend.

spell.name: Friendfetch
spell.description: You shoot out ephemeral, telekinetic strands that drag each target directly toward you, stopping in the closest unoccupied space to you in this path. This is forced movement.

spell.name: Grease
spell.targets: 4 contiguous 5-foot squares OR 1 object of 1 Bulk or less
spell.description: You conjure grease.  All solid ground in the area is covered with grease. Each creature standing on the greasy surface must succeed at a Reflex save or an Acrobatics check against your spell DC or fall prone. Creatures using an action to move onto the greasy surface during the spell's duration must attempt either a Reflex save or an Acrobatics check to Balance. A creature that Steps or Crawls doesn't have to attempt a check or save. Alternatively, if you Cast the Spell on an unattended object, anyone trying to pick up the object must succeed at an Acrobatics check or Reflex save against your spell DC to do so. If you target an attended object, the creature that has the object must attempt an Acrobatics check or Reflex save. On a failure, the holder or wielder takes a –2 circumstance penalty to all checks that involve using the object; on a critical failure, the holder or wielder releases the item. The object lands in an adjacent square of the GM's choice. If you Cast this Spell on a worn object, the wearer gains a +2 circumstance bonus to Fortitude saves against attempts to grapple them.

spell.name: Guidance
spell.duration: Until the start of your next turn
spell.description: You ask for the guidance of supernatural entities, granting the target a +1 status bonus to one attack roll, Perception check, saving throw, or skill check the target attempts before the duration ends. The target chooses which roll to use the bonus on before rolling. If the target uses the bonus, the spell ends. Either way, the target is then temporarily immune for 1 hour.

spell.name: Haunting Hymn
spell.description: You echo a jarring hymn that only creatures in the area can hear. The hymn deals 1d8 sonic damage, with a basic Fortitude save. If a target critically fails the save, it's also deafened for 1 minute.

spell.name: Hymn of Healing
spell.description: Your glorious singing mends wounds and provides a temporary respite from harm. The target gains fast healing 2. When you Cast the Spell and the first time each round you Sustain the Spell, the target gains 2 temporary Hit Points, which last for 1 round.

heightened.+1: The fast healing and temporary Hit Points each increase by 2.

spell.name: Illusory Creature
spell.description: You create an illusory image of a Large or smaller creature. It generates the appropriate sounds, smells, and feels believable to the touch. If you and the image are ever farther than 500 feet apart, the spell ends. The image can't speak, but you can use your actions to speak through the creature, with the spell disguising your voice as appropriate. You might need to attempt a Deception or Performance check to mimic the creature, as determined by the GM. This is especially likely if you're trying to imitate a specific person and engage with someone that person knows. In combat, the illusion can use 2 actions per turn, which it uses when you Sustain the spell. It uses your spell attack modifier for attack rolls and your spell DC for its AC. Its saving throw modifiers are equal to your spell DC – 10. It is substantial enough that it can flank other creatures. If the image is hit by an attack or fails a save, the spell ends. The illusion can cause damage by making the target believe the illusion's attacks are real, but it cannot otherwise directly affect the physical world. If the illusory creature hits with a Strike, the target takes 3d4 mental damage. The illusion's Strikes are nonlethal. If the damage doesn't correspond to the image of the monster—for example, if an illusory Large dragon deals only 5 damage—the GM might allow the target to attempt an immediate Perception check to disbelieve the spell. Any relevant resistances and weaknesses apply if the target thinks they do, as judged by the GM. For example, if the illusion wields a warhammer and attacks a creature resistant to bludgeoning damage, the creature would take less mental damage. However, illusory damage does not deactivate regeneration or trigger other effects that require a certain damage type. The GM should track illusory damage dealt by the illusion. Any creature that touches the image or uses the Seek action to examine it can attempt to disbelieve your illusion. When a creature disbelieves the illusion, it recovers from half the damage it had taken from it (if any) and doesn't take any further damage from it.

heightened.+1: The damage of the image's Strikes increases by 1d4, and the maximum size of creature you can create increases by one (to a maximum of Gargantuan).

spell.name: Illusory Object
spell.description: You create an illusory visual image of a stationary object. The entire image must fit within the spell's area. The object appears to animate naturally, but it doesn't make sounds or generate smells. For example, water would appear to pour down an illusory waterfall, but it would be silent. Any creature that touches the image or uses the Seek action to examine it can attempt to disbelieve your illusion.

heightened.2nd: our image makes appropriate sounds, generates normal smells, and feels right to the touch. The spell gains the auditory and olfactory traits. The duration increases to 1 hour.
heightened.5th: As the 2nd-rank version, but the duration is unlimited.

spell.name: Infectious Enthusiasm
spell.description: With enchanted gusto, you encourage yourself to get things done and share your motivation with your allies. You gain a +1 status bonus to your choice of attack rolls, Will saves, or Charisma-based skill checks. During the spell's duration, an ally who observed you Casting this Spell and was within 30 feet when you did so can take a single action, which has the concentrate trait, to gain the same bonus you chose until the start of its next turn. Using this spell to influence someone in a social situation is typically considered a faux pas, negating the benefit on Charisma-based skills if the subject of the check observed you casting the spell.

spell.name: Inner Upheaval
spell.description: You focus your qi into devastating magical attacks. Make an unarmed Strike or Flurry of Blows (this doesn't change the limit on using only one flourish per turn). You gain a +1 status bonus to your attack rolls with the Strikes, and the Strikes deal 1d6 extra damage. This damage can be any of the following types of your choice, chosen each time you Strike: force, spirit, vitality, or void.

spell.name: Inspire Courage
spell.description: You inspire your allies with words or tunes of encouragement. You and all allies in the area gain a +1 status bonus to attack rolls, damage rolls, and saves against fear effects.

spell.name: Instant Parade
spell.description: An illusory parade with dozens of participants and performers appears around you, following you as you move. The parade is lively and noisy, providing enough cover and distraction to hide among the crowd. You and other creatures can Hide and Sneak inside the crowd, though creatures that disbelieve the illusion still see creatures within as normal. You can choose to send the parade off with a 2-action activity, which has the concentrate trait. When you do so, the parade no longer follows you and instead continues traveling in the direction of your choice. The parade travels 100 feet over 1 round and then disappears as the spell is Dismissed. You can otherwise Dismiss the spell normally if you prefer.

spell.name: Knock
spell.description: You make the target easier to open. Knock grants a +4 status bonus to any creature that tries to open the target door, lock, or container with an Athletics or a Thievery check. You can attempt a Thievery check to open the target as part of casting knock, and you add your level even if you're untrained. Knock counteracts Lock.

spell.name: Light
spell.duration: Until the next time you make your daily preparations
spell.description: You create an orb of light that sheds bright light in a 20-foot radius (and dim light for the next 20 feet) in a color you choose. If you create the light in the same space as a willing creature, you can attach the light to the creature, causing it to float near that creature as it moves. You can Sustain the spell to move the light up to 60 feet; you can attach or detach it from a creature as part of this movement. You can Dismiss the spell. If you Cast the Spell while you already have four light spells active, you must choose one of the existing spells to end.

heightened.4th: The orb sheds light in a 60-foot radius (and dim light for the next 60 feet).

spell.name: Lingering Composition
spell.description: You add a flourish to your composition to extend its benefits. If your next action is to cast a cantrip composition with a duration of 1 round, attempt a Performance check. The DC is standard-difficulty of a level equal to the highestt level target of your composition.

degrees.Failure: The composition lasts 1 round, but you don't expend a focus point for trying.

spell.name: Mind Games
spell.description: You lock minds with the target, trying to mentally subdue them. The target must attempt a Will save. Each time you Sustain this Spell, the target attempts another Will save.

degrees.Critical Success: You are stunned 1 and the spell ends.

spell.name: Mirror Image
spell.description: Three illusory images of you swirl about your space, potentially causing those who attack you to hit one of the images instead of you. Any attack that would hit you has a random chance of hitting one of your images instead of you. If all three images remain, there is a 1 in 4 chance of hitting you (1 on 1d4). With two images remaining, there is a 1 in 3 chance of hitting you (1–2 on 1d6). With only one image, the chances are 1 in 2 (1–3 on 1d6). Once an image is hit, it is destroyed. If an attack roll fails to hit your AC but doesn't critically fail, it destroys an image but has no additional effect (even if the attack would normally have an effect on a failure). If an attack roll is a critical success and would hit one of the images, one of the images is destroyed and the attack roll becomes a success against you. Once all the images are destroyed, the spell ends.

spell.name: Murmured Prayer (+2 Guidance)
spell.description: You utter a prayer to a particularly relevant deity as part of casting Guidance: the target gains a +2 status bonus instead of +1 to one attack roll, Perception check, saving throw, or skill check before the start of your next turn. If the deity's goals do not align with your own, they may refuse to aid you. The target is immune to further Guidance for 1 hour either way.

spell.name: Mystic Armor
spell.duration: Until the next time you make your daily preparations
spell.description: You ward yourself with shimmering magical energy, gaining a +1 item bonus to AC and a maximum Dexterity modifier of +5. While wearing mystic armor, you use your unarmored proficiency to calculate your AC.

heightened.4th: You gain a +1 item bonus to saving throws.
heightened.6th: The item bonus to AC increases to +2, and you gain a +1 item bonus to saving throws.
heightened.8th: The item bonus to AC increases to +2, and you gain a +2 item bonus to saving throws.
heightened.10th: The item bonus to AC increases to +3, and you gain a +3 item bonus to saving throws.

spell.name: Noise Blast
spell.description: A cacophonous noise blasts out, dealing 2d10 sonic damage. Each creature must attempt a Fortitude save.

degrees.Failure: The target takes full damage and is Deafened for 1 round
degrees.Critical Failure: The creature takes double damage and is deafened for 1 minute, and stunned 1.

spell.name: Overselling Flourish
spell.description: You make a grand spectacle out of getting hit. Enhanced by magic, this spectacle features sprays of blood, anguished screams, or other theatrics that appear to result from your foe's attack. The triggering creature must attempt a Will saving throw.

degrees.Success: The creature is thrown off by your display. The creature becomes dazzled until the start of your turn.
degrees.Failure: The creature fully believes your performance, leaving itself open. The creature becomes dazzled and flat-footed until the start of your turn.
degrees.Critical Failure: The creature is enraptured by the display. The creature uses its remaining actions to watch you in awe. It then remains dazzled and flat-footed until the start of your turn.

spell.name: Pocket Library
spell.description: When you Cast this Spell, choose any skill in which you are at least trained that has the Recall Knowledge action. During the duration of this spell, you can call forth a tome from an extradimensional library called the Akashic Record when attempting a Recall Knowledge check using your chosen skill. This is part of the action to Recall Knowledge. You must have a hand free to do so. The tome appears in your hand, open to an appropriate page. This grants you a +1 status bonus to the Recall Knowledge check. If you roll a critical failure on this check, you get a failure instead. If the roll is successful and the subject is a creature, you gain additional information or context about the creature. Once you reference a book from your pocket library, the spell ends.

heightened.3rd: The status bonus increases to +2 and you can reference your pocket library twice before the spell ends.
heightened.6th: The status bonus increases to +3 and you can reference your pocket library three times before the spell ends.
heightened.9th: The status bonus increases to +4 and you can reference your pocket library four times before the spell ends.

spell.name: Prestidigitation
spell.description: The simplest magic does your bidding. You can perform simple magical effects for as long as you Sustain the Spell. Each time you Sustain the Spell, you can choose any one of four options: cook, lift, clean, or color. Prestidigitation can't deal damage or cause adverse conditions. Any actual change to an object (beyond what is noted above) persists only as long as you Sustain the spell.

spell.name: Ray of Frost
spell.description: You blast an icy ray. Make a spell attack roll. The ray deals 2d4 cold damage.

degrees.Critical Success: The target takes double damage and takes a –10-foot status penalty to its Speeds for 1 round.

spell.name: Runic Body
spell.description: Glowing runes appear on the target's body. All its unarmed attacks become +1 striking unarmed attacks, gaining a +1 item bonus to attack rolls and increasing the number of damage dice to two.

heightened.6th: The unarmed attacks are +2 greater striking.
heightened.9th: The unarmed attacks are +3 major striking.

spell.name: Serrate
spell.description: With a pinch, the metal of a weapon twists, adding numerous jagged blades along its surface. Strikes with the target weapon deal an additional 1d4 slashing damage until the start of your next turn.

spell.name: Shield
spell.duration: Until the start of your next turn
spell.description: You raise a magical shield of force. This counts as using the Raise a Shield action, giving you a +1 circumstance bonus to AC until the start of your next turn, but it doesn't require a hand to use. While the spell is in effect, you can use the Shield Block reaction with your magical shield. The shield has Hardness 5. You can use the spell's reaction to reduce damage from any spell or magical effect, even if it doesn't deal physical damage. After you use Shield Block, the spell ends and you can't cast it again for 10 minutes.

spell.name: Shields of the Spirit
spell.description:
You Raise your Shield, causing ephemeral spirit shields to float within your champion's aura. The shields last until the start of your next turn or until you're no longer raising your shield, whichever comes first. While one of your allies is in your champion's aura, the shields grant them a +1 status bonus to AC, and each time an enemy makes an attack against the ally, the enemy takes 1d4 spirit damage (even if it misses).
The benefit applies only while an ally is in your aura, ending for any ally that leaves and applying to any that enters later. As normal, you don't count as your own ally and therefore don't get the benefits of the spirit shields yourself.

spell.name: Shocking Grasp
spell.description: You shroud your hands in a crackling field of electricity. Make a melee spell attack roll. On a hit, the target takes 2d12 electricity damage. If the target is wearing metal armor or is made of metal, you gain a +1 circumstance bonus to your attack roll with shocking grasp, and the target also takes 1d4 persistent electricity damage on a hit.

heightened.+1: The damage increases by 1d12, and the persistent electricity damage increases by 1.

riders.label: Metal armor: persistent electricity
riders.note: Only if the target wears metal armor or is made of metal (you also get +1 on the attack roll)

spell.name: Sleep
spell.description: Each creature in the area becomes drowsy, possibly nodding off. A creature that falls unconscious from this spell doesn't fall prone or release what it's holding. This spell doesn't prevent creatures from waking up due to a successful Perception check, limiting its utility in combat.

degrees.Success: The creature takes a –1 status penalty to Perception checks for 1 round.
degrees.Failure: The creature falls unconscious. If it's still unconscious after 1 minute, it wakes up automatically.
degrees.Critical Failure: The creature falls unconscious. If it's still unconscious after 1 hour, it wakes up automatically.

heightened.4th: The creatures fall unconscious for 1 round on a failure or 1 minute on a critical failure. They fall prone and release what they're holding, and they can't attempt Perception checks to wake up. When the duration ends, the creature is sleeping normally instead of automatically waking up.

spell.name: Sudden Bolt
spell.description: You call down a small bolt of lightning on the target, dealing 4d12 electricity damage.

spell.name: Summon Undead
spell.description: You summon a creature that has the undead trait and whose level is –1 to fight for you.

heightened.2nd: The creature can be up to Level 1.
heightened.3rd: The creature can be up to Level 2.
heightened.4th: The creature can be up to Level 3.
heightened.5th: The creature can be up to Level 5.
heightened.6th: The creature can be up to Level 7.
heightened.7th: The creature can be up to Level 9.
heightened.8th: The creature can be up to Level 11.
heightened.9th: The creature can be up to Level 13.
heightened.10th: The creature can be up to Level 15.

spell.name: Telekinetic Hand
spell.targets: 1 unattended object of Light Bulk or less
spell.description: You create a floating, magical hand, either invisible or ghostlike, that grasps the target object and levitates it slowly up to 20 feet in any direction. When you Sustain the spell, you can move the object an additional 20 feet. If the object is in the air when the spell ends, the object falls.

heightened.3rd: You can target an unattended object with a Bulk of 1 or less.
heightened.5th: The range increases to 60 feet, and you can target an unattended object with a Bulk of 1 or less.
heightened.7th: The range increases to 60 feet, and you can target an unattended object with a Bulk of 2 or less.

spell.name: Telekinetic Projectile
